"<div>What is <b>motion parallax&nbsp;</b>(monocular visual)?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""When&#x20;moving&#x20;&#x28;like&#x20;in&#x20;a&#x20;train&#x29;&#x2C;&#x20;obects&#x20;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;further&#x20;away&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;move&#x20;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;slower&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;than&#x20;closer&#x20;objects&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div>What is <b>motion parallax&nbsp;</b>(monocular visual)?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">When moving (like in a train), obects <i><u>further away</u></i> move <i><u>slower</u></i> than closer objects.</span><br></div>

"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Size&#x20;constancy&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is knowing two objects are the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>same </i>size</span>&nbsp;despite the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>closer </i>one</span> appearing larger.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Size constancy</b></span> is knowing two objects are the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>same </i>size</span>&nbsp;despite the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>closer </i>one</span> appearing larger.</div>

"<div>Our ears adapt to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;prolonged&#x2C;&#x20;loud&#x20;sounds&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> by <i>constricting</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">tympanic membrane</b></span>.</div>"	"<div>Our ears adapt to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">prolonged, loud sounds</u></span> by <i>constricting</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">tympanic membrane</b></span>.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This <i>only</i>&nbsp;works for prolonged loud noises - NOT something like a gunshot.</div></div>"
"In <u>sensory adaptation</u>, how does our <b>sense of touch&nbsp;</b>adapt to temperature?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Thermoreceptors&#x20;in&#x20;our&#x20;hands&#x20;become&#x20;&lt;i&gt;desensitized&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;and&#x20;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;stop&#x20;firing&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;after&#x20;some&#x20;time&#x2E;&#x20;We&#x20;perceive&#x2E;&#x2E;&#x2E;&#x20;water&#x20;feels&#x20;hot&amp;nbsp&#x3B;→&#x20;water&#x20;&lt;i&gt;stops&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;feeling&#x20;hot&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"In <u>sensory adaptation</u>, how does our <b>sense of touch&nbsp;</b>adapt to temperature?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Thermoreceptors in our hands become <i>desensitized</i> and <i><u>stop firing</u></i> after some time. We perceive... water feels hot&nbsp;→ water <i>stops </i>feeling hot.</span><br></div>

What happened next?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Their eyes eventually <i>adapted </i>to the goggles and <u><i>flipped vision back</i></u> to the proper orientation.</span><br></div>

"In <u>sensory adaptation</u>, how is our <b>vision</b>&nbsp;able to adapt to changes in light?<div><br></div><div><i>Bright </i>outside... pupils <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""constrict"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div><i>Dark </i>outside... pupils <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""dilate"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"In <u>sensory adaptation</u>, how is our <b>vision</b>&nbsp;able to adapt to changes in light?<div><br></div><div><i>Bright </i>outside... pupils <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">constrict</span></div><div><i>Dark </i>outside... pupils <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">dilate</span></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Pupils constricting&nbsp;→ allows <i>less </i>light to get in<div>Pupils dilating&nbsp;→ allows <i>more </i>light to get in</div></div>"
"<div>In <b>Weber's Law</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""just&#x2D;noticeable&#x20;difference"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when you first notice a <u><i>change </i>in stimulus intensity</u>.<br></div>"	"<div>In <b>Weber's Law</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">just-noticeable difference</span> is when you first notice a <u><i>change </i>in stimulus intensity</u>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>2.2 lbs is just past the just-noticable difference for you recognize that a weight has been swapped.</div><div><br></div><img src=""2f00bef0b16b6327e97c130148b24580.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Weber&#x27;s&#x20;Law&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> says that the magnitude of the <u>just-noticable difference</u> for a stimulus is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">always <i>constant</i></span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Weber's Law</b></span> says that the magnitude of the <u>just-noticable difference</u> for a stimulus is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">always <i>constant</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- ""Approximately"" because this is not set in stone.</div><div>- 2.2 lbs is just past the just-noticable difference for you recognize that a weight has been swapped.</div><div><br></div><img src=""2f00bef0b16b6327e97c130148b24580.png""></div>"
"What is the <b>absolute threshold of sensation</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""The&#x20;&lt;i&gt;minimum&#x20;intensity&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;of&#x20;a&#x20;stimulus&#x20;needed&#x20;for&#x20;you&#x20;to&#x20;&lt;i&gt;detect&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;its&#x20;presence&#x20;&lt;u&gt;50&#x25;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;time&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"What is the <b>absolute threshold of sensation</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">The <i>minimum intensity</i> of a stimulus needed for you to <i>detect </i>its presence <u>50%</u> of the time.</span><br></div>

"<div>In terms of the <u>absolute threshold of sensation</u>, what is&nbsp;<b>subliminal stimuli</b>?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Stimuli&#x20;that&#x20;falls&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;below&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;the&#x20;absolute&#x20;threshold&#x20;&#x2D;&#x20;the&#x20;stimulus&#x20;is&#x20;there&#x20;but&#x20;we&#x20;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;won&#x27;t&#x20;reliably&#x20;detect&#x20;it&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div>In terms of the <u>absolute threshold of sensation</u>, what is&nbsp;<b>subliminal stimuli</b>?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Stimuli that falls&nbsp;<i>below </i>the absolute threshold - the stimulus is there but we <i><u>won't reliably detect it</u></i>.</span><br></div>

"What is <b>signal detection theory</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Whether&#x20;or&#x20;not&#x20;we&#x27;re&#x20;able&#x20;to&#x20;detect&#x20;a&#x20;signal&#x20;under&#x20;conditions&#x20;of&#x20;&lt;i&gt;uncertainty&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"What is <b>signal detection theory</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Whether or not we're able to detect a signal under conditions of <i>uncertainty</i></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div>In signal detection theory, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;conservative&#x20;strategies&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are when you <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>always</i> <u>say no</u></span> unless you're <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">100% confident</span> the signal <i>is</i> there.</div>"	"<div>In signal detection theory, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>conservative strategies</b></span> are when you <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>always</i> <u>say no</u></span> unless you're <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">100% confident</span> the signal <i>is</i> there.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Good thing → minimizes false positives<br><div>Bad thing → lots of misses.</div></div>"
"<div>In signal detection theory, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;liberal&#x20;strategies&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are when you <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>always</i>&nbsp;<u>say yes</u></span>, the signal <i>is</i> there.<br></div>"	"<div>In signal detection theory, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>liberal strategies</b></span> are when you <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>always</i>&nbsp;<u>say yes</u></span>, the signal <i>is</i> there.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Good thing → you will <i>always </i>detect its presence<br><div>Bad thing → lots of false positives.</div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Bottom&#x2D;up&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>processing</b> is when our perception of something&nbsp;<i>is not</i> based on background information - we <u>take it <i>as is</i></u>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Bottom-up</b></span> <b>processing</b> is when our perception of something&nbsp;<i>is not</i> based on background information - we <u>take it <i>as is</i></u>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

This is more ""data-driven""<div><br></div><div><img src=""a31c69cd58935e603a79d85269cf275a.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Top&#x2D;down&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>processing</b> is when our <u>background experience</u> <i>influences</i> our perception of stimuli.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Top-down</b></span> <b>processing</b> is when our <u>background experience</u> <i>influences</i> our perception of stimuli.<br></div>

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><br></div><div>The <u>choroid</u> absorbs the remaining light due to its black pigment.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""eye-anatomy-700x635.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"What allows cats to have <i><u>night-vision</u></i>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""After&#x20;light&#x20;passes&#x20;&lt;i&gt;through&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;retina&#x2C;&#x20;it&#x20;is&#x20;&lt;i&gt;reflected&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;by&#x20;the&#x20;choroid&#x20;&lt;i&gt;back&#x20;onto&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;retina&#x20;for&#x20;&quot;another&#x20;go&#x2E;&quot;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"What allows cats to have <i><u>night-vision</u></i>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">After light passes <i>through</i> the retina, it is <i>reflected</i> by the choroid <i>back onto</i> the retina for ""another go.""</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>In cats, the choroid is shiny (in <i>contrast</i> with humans' black-pigmented choroid).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""eye-anatomy-700x635.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In the eye, what is the <i>function</i> of the <b>fovea</b>?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Allows&#x20;you&#x20;to&#x20;see&#x20;sharp&#x20;detail"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>In the eye, what is the <i>function</i> of the <b>fovea</b>?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Allows you to see sharp detail</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Arises from its high <i><u>cone</u></i> density.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""eye-anatomy-700x635.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In the eye, the <b>macula</b> is a smal, cone-dense region. What <u><i>dimple-like structure</i></u> does it have in its center?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Fovea"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>In the eye, the <b>macula</b> is a smal, cone-dense region. What <u><i>dimple-like structure</i></u> does it have in its center?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Fovea</span></div>

"With vision, what do <b>photoreceptors</b> do?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Take&#x20;&lt;u&gt;light&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;and&#x20;&lt;i&gt;convert&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;it&#x20;into&#x20;&lt;u&gt;neural&#x20;impulses&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"With vision, what do <b>photoreceptors</b> do?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Take <u>light</u> and <i>convert</i> it into <u>neural impulses</u>.</span><br></div>

</div>"
"The <b>retina</b> contains <i>both</i> <u>rods</u> <i>and</i> <u>cones</u>. <i>Where</i> are the rods &amp; cones <i>located</i>&nbsp;(respectively)&nbsp;on the retina?<div><br></div><div>On the retina...</div><div><br></div><div><u>Center</u>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""cones"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div><u>Periphery</u>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""rods"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"The <b>retina</b> contains <i>both</i> <u>rods</u> <i>and</i> <u>cones</u>. <i>Where</i> are the rods &amp; cones <i>located</i>&nbsp;(respectively)&nbsp;on the retina?<div><br></div><div>On the retina...</div><div><br></div><div><u>Center</u>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">cones</span></div><div><u>Periphery</u>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">rods</span></div>

"<div>The <b style="""">retina </b>contains <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""many&#x20;&lt;i&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[more/less]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">rods</span> than <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cones</span>.</div>"	"<div>The <b style="""">retina </b>contains <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">many <i>more</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">rods</span> than <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cones</span>.</div>

"<div><u>Activation</u> of the <b>phototransduction cascade</b> turns the rod <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""off"" data-ordinal=""1"">[on/off]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><u>Activation</u> of the <b>phototransduction cascade</b> turns the rod <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">off</span>.<br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""487fb6358da3a4834dfa65b6469f5dbd.png""></div>"
"<i>After</i> a rod is turned <i>off</i> by the <u>phototransduction cascade</u>, what happens?<div><br></div><div><b>Rod</b> turns <i><u>off</u></i>&nbsp;→ turns <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""bipolar&#x20;cell"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <i>on</i> → turns <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""retinal&#x20;ganglion&#x20;cell"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <i>on</i> → <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""optic&#x20;nerve"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> → brain</div>"	"<i>After</i> a rod is turned <i>off</i> by the <u>phototransduction cascade</u>, what happens?<div><br></div><div><b>Rod</b> turns <i><u>off</u></i>&nbsp;→ turns <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">bipolar cell</span> <i>on</i> → turns <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">retinal ganglion cell</span> <i>on</i> → <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">optic nerve</span> → brain</div>

"<div>In the rod, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;rhodopsin&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[protein]</span> uses its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""vitamin&#x20;A&#x20;&#x28;retinal&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[cofactor]</span> cofactor to <i>interact</i> with UV light.</div>"	"<div>In the rod, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>rhodopsin</b></span> uses its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">vitamin A (retinal)</span> cofactor to <i>interact</i> with UV light.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Rhodopsin is the protein &amp; retinal is its cofactor. Recall that vitamins are very commonly used as cofactors.</div><div><br></div><img src=""cfdd19b384f67c93a9f71006f4ebbe6c.png""></div>"
"<div>Once UV light changes <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;rhodopsin&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[protein]</span>'s conformation, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">transducin</span>'s&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>α-subunit</i></span> binds to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">phosphodiesterase</span>.</div>"	"<div>Once UV light changes <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>rhodopsin</b></span>'s conformation, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">transducin</span>'s&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>α-subunit</i></span> binds to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">phosphodiesterase</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Transducin is the name of the G-protein on rhodopsin.</div><div>- Phosphodiesterase converts cGMP → GMP to close Na+ channels.</div><div><br></div><img src=""b7c4c92d16504c738f7d67ed6e6605e4.png""></div>"
"<div>In the <u>phosotransduction cascade</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;phosphodiesterase&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[enzyme]</span> catalyzes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cGMP</span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">GMP</span> - <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>closing</i></span> Na<sup>+</sup> channels.</div>"	"<div>In the <u>phosotransduction cascade</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>phosphodiesterase</b></span> catalyzes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cGMP</span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">GMP</span> - <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>closing</i></span> Na<sup>+</sup> channels.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Na<sup>+</sup> channels are held <u>open</u> by <b>cGMP</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""04b1e45463d5912ed6b62a1232fe0458.png""></div>"
"<div><div>In the <u>phototransduction cascade</u>, what happens <i>after</i>&nbsp;cGMP is converted <i>into </i><b>GMP</b> by <u>phosphodiesterase</u>?</div></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Na&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x20;channels&#x20;&lt;i&gt;close&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;rod&#x20;is&#x20;turned&#x20;&lt;i&gt;off&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div><div>In the <u>phototransduction cascade</u>, what happens <i>after</i>&nbsp;cGMP is converted <i>into </i><b>GMP</b> by <u>phosphodiesterase</u>?</div></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Na<sup>+</sup> channels <i>close</i> &amp; rod is turned <i>off</i></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""346e0eed9e96224adb251a43e5cb6cff.png""></div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<b>Rhodopsin</b> is the protein present in <u>rods</u>. <div>This area does <i>not</i>&nbsp;contain any photopigments.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""548a379f8f3cf3ec5901540495ac5f10.png""><br></div></div>"
"In the eye, the <u>fovea</u> is <i>dimpled</i> inward. What <i>benefit</i> does this serve?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Allows&#x20;light&#x20;to&#x20;come&#x20;into&#x20;&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;direct&#x20;contact&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;with&#x20;cones&#x20;instead&#x20;of&#x20;having&#x20;to&#x20;pass&#x20;through&#x20;nerve&#x20;axons"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"In the eye, the <u>fovea</u> is <i>dimpled</i> inward. What <i>benefit</i> does this serve?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Allows light to come into <u><i>direct contact</i></u> with cones instead of having to pass through nerve axons</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Otherwise, light would have to pass through bundles of axons.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""d2200239d908ddd6900805e9d36e2f3b.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Signals in the&nbsp;<b>optic nerve</b>&nbsp;on the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""nasal"" data-ordinal=""1"">[nasal/temproal]</span> side <i>cross-over</i> the <u>optic chiasm</u> to reach their desired half of the brain.</div>"	"<div>Signals in the&nbsp;<b>optic nerve</b>&nbsp;on the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">nasal</span> side <i>cross-over</i> the <u>optic chiasm</u> to reach their desired half of the brain.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Optic nerve</b>: carries both signals (green &amp; purple) before reaching the optic chiasm.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Optic tract</b>: carries the ""organized"" signals (right with right, left with left) past the optic chiasm to reach the brain.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""800875_376799_ans_9c7e4b4ae89d4c7ea75005226f5339e1.jpg""></div><div></div></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;optic&#x20;chiasm&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[structure]</span> is the place at which <i>signals </i>from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>optic nerves</u></span> from our left &amp; right eyes <i>cross-over</i> to reach different halves of the brain.<br></div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>optic chiasm</b></span> is the place at which <i>signals </i>from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>optic nerves</u></span> from our left &amp; right eyes <i>cross-over</i> to reach different halves of the brain.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Optic nerve</b>: carries both signals (green &amp; purple) before reaching the optic chiasm.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Optic tract</b>: carries the ""organized"" signals (right with right, left with left) past the optic chiasm to reach the brain.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""800875_376799_ans_9c7e4b4ae89d4c7ea75005226f5339e1.jpg""></div></div>"
"There are <i>three</i> different <i>types</i> of <b>cones</b> - RGB. Rank each time from <u><i>most to least numerous</i></u>.<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;R&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&#x28;60&#x25;&#x29;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;→&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;b&gt;G&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;&#x28;30&#x25;&#x29;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;→&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;b&gt;B&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;&#x28;10&#x25;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"There are <i>three</i> different <i>types</i> of <b>cones</b> - RGB. Rank each time from <u><i>most to least numerous</i></u>.<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>R</b>&nbsp;(60%)&nbsp;→&nbsp;<b>G</b> (30%)&nbsp;→&nbsp;<b>B</b> (10%)</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;trichromatic&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span> of color vision says cones corresponding to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">red</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">green</span>, and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">blue</span> are responsible for the detection of&nbsp;<i>all</i> colors.</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>trichromatic theory</b></span> of color vision says cones corresponding to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">red</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">green</span>, and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">blue</span> are responsible for the detection of&nbsp;<i>all</i> colors.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Also called <b>Young-Helmholtz theory</b>.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-f1546c94471220b589f3378b47fe8a535da855e8.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;parvo&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b>cellular pathway </b>detects general shape, fine detail, and color of an object in visual processing.</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>parvo</b></span><b>cellular pathway </b>detects general shape, fine detail, and color of an object in visual processing.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Parvo&nbsp;→ <b>Spatial resolution</b><div><b><br></b></div><div><img src=""81f26403eb90220b10ca8acd1a0881d4.png""><b><br></b></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fetch-4ca67100e766acf9154240e68809d7480d0c054d.php""><br></div></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;magno&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[parvo/magno]</span><b>cellular</b>&nbsp;<b>pathway</b> tells us the object's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>motion</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>depth</u></span>.</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>magno</b></span><b>cellular</b>&nbsp;<b>pathway</b> tells us the object's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>motion</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>depth</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'

"What is the <i>outermost</i>, visible portion of the <u>ear</u> called?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Pinna"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"What is the <i>outermost</i>, visible portion of the <u>ear</u> called?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Pinna</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div>The <b style="""">auditory canal</b> is <i>also</i> called the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;external&#x20;auditory&#x20;meatus&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div>The <b style="""">auditory canal</b> is <i>also</i> called the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">external auditory meatus</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"In the ear, once a sound wave reaches the <u>tympanic membrane</u>, it causes it to <i>vibrate </i>and ""shake"" <i>3</i> smaller bones. What <i>are </i>these <u><i>3 bones</i></u>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Malleus&lt;div&gt;Incus&lt;&#x2F;div&gt;&lt;div&gt;Stapes"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div></div>"	"In the ear, once a sound wave reaches the <u>tympanic membrane</u>, it causes it to <i>vibrate </i>and ""shake"" <i>3</i> smaller bones. What <i>are </i>these <u><i>3 bones</i></u>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Malleus<div>Incus</div><div>Stapes</span></div></div>

"<div>Vibration of the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;stapes&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[bone]</span> <i>presses in</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>oval window</u></span>, focing fluid in the cochlea to <i>spiral around</i>.&nbsp;</div>"	"<div>Vibration of the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">stapes</span></span> <i>presses in</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>oval window</u></span>, focing fluid in the cochlea to <i>spiral around</i>.&nbsp;</div>

"In our ear, <b>hair cells</b> are composed of <i>kinocilia</i>&nbsp;(connected via tip links). When kinocilia move, <i>how </i>does this lead to the <i>opening </i>of <b>K<sup>+</sup> channels</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Kinocilia&#x20;movement&#x20;cause&#x20;tip&#x20;links&#x20;to&#x20;&lt;i&gt;stretch&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;which&amp;nbsp&#x3B;are&#x20;connected&#x20;to&#x20;&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;style&#x3A;&#x20;italic&#x3B;&quot;&gt;mechanically&#x2D;gated&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;style&#x3A;&#x20;italic&#x3B;&quot;&gt;&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;K&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x20;channels&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"In our ear, <b>hair cells</b> are composed of <i>kinocilia</i>&nbsp;(connected via tip links). When kinocilia move, <i>how </i>does this lead to the <i>opening </i>of <b>K<sup>+</sup> channels</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Kinocilia movement cause tip links to <i>stretch</i>, which&nbsp;are connected to <u style=""font-style: italic;"">mechanically-gated</u><span style=""font-style: italic;""> </span>K<sup>+</sup> channels.</span><br></div>

"<i>After </i>mechanically-gated K<sup>+</sup> channels <i>open </i>on the kinocilia...<div><br></div><div>K<sup>+</sup> influx <i>induces</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Ca&lt;sup&gt;2&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x20;influx&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;→ action potential stimulates <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>spiral ganglion</b> cell</span>&nbsp;→ reaches <b>brain</b>.</div>"	"<i>After </i>mechanically-gated K<sup>+</sup> channels <i>open </i>on the kinocilia...<div><br></div><div>K<sup>+</sup> influx <i>induces</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ca<sup>2+</sup> influx</b></span>&nbsp;→ action potential stimulates <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>spiral ganglion</b> cell</span>&nbsp;→ reaches <b>brain</b>.</div>

"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Place&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> refers to how sound waves with <i>different</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>frequencies</u></span> stimulate <i>different</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>hair cells</u></span> in the cochlea - causing us to hear different sounds.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Place theory</b></span> refers to how sound waves with <i>different</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>frequencies</u></span> stimulate <i>different</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>hair cells</u></span> in the cochlea - causing us to hear different sounds.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Also known as <b>basilar tuning</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""61b929a39c284b54933dae46d3f8667f.png""><br></div>"
"<div><b>Place theory</b> is <i>also</i> called <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""basilar&#x20;tuning"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><b>Place theory</b> is <i>also</i> called <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">basilar tuning</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Says we hear different sounds depending on the specific hair cells that get stimulated along the basilar membrane.</div><div>- Sound waves with longer wavelengths travel further and stimulate hair cells further towards the apex of the cochlea.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""61b929a39c284b54933dae46d3f8667f.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Sensorineural hearing loss</b> is a condition that results in ""nerve deafness."" In the auditory <u><i>process</i></u>, <i>which step </i>fails?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Normal&#x20;&lt;i&gt;conversion&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;of&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;movement&#x20;of&#x20;fluid&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;in&#x20;the&#x20;cochlea&#x20;into&#x20;an&#x20;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;action&#x20;potential&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;did&#x20;&lt;b&gt;not&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;work&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<b>Sensorineural hearing loss</b> is a condition that results in ""nerve deafness."" In the auditory <u><i>process</i></u>, <i>which step </i>fails?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Normal <i>conversion </i>of the <u><i>movement of fluid</i></u> in the cochlea into an <i><u>action potential</u></i> did <b>not</b> work.</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Marked as an <b>X</b> below.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""b73ff632aa8e0c63d48f2022a58f8958.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Sensorineural hearing loss</b> is a <i>failure </i>to convert fluid movement within the cochlea into an action potential. What <i><u>device</u></i> fixes this?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Cochlear&#x20;implant"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<b>Sensorineural hearing loss</b> is a <i>failure </i>to convert fluid movement within the cochlea into an action potential. What <i><u>device</u></i> fixes this?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Cochlear implant</span></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Failure is marked as an <b>X</b> below.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""b73ff632aa8e0c63d48f2022a58f8958.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In a <u>cochlear implant</u>, what does the <b>speech processor</b> do?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Takes&#x20;the&#x20;sound&#x20;wave&#x20;and&#x20;converts&#x20;it&#x20;into&#x20;an&#x20;electrical&#x20;impulse"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"In a <u>cochlear implant</u>, what does the <b>speech processor</b> do?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Takes the sound wave and converts it into an electrical impulse</span></div>

"<div>What is the <b><i>labelled lines model</i></b>?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;Each&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;axon&#x20;in&#x20;a&#x20;taste&#x20;receptor&#x20;&#x28;in&#x20;a&#x20;taste&#x20;bud&#x29;&#x20;travels&#x20;&#x28;&lt;i&gt;without&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;synapsing&#x29;&#x20;to&#x20;a&#x20;&lt;i&gt;different&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;part&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;b&gt;gustatory&#x20;cortex&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div>What is the <b><i>labelled lines model</i></b>?</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>Each</i> axon in a taste receptor (in a taste bud) travels (<i>without</i> synapsing) to a <i>different</i> part of the <b>gustatory cortex</b></span><br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"During REM sleep, the activity of the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;pre&#x2D;frontal&#x20;cortex&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreased</i></span>&nbsp;- causing <u>non-sensical dreams</u>."	"During REM sleep, the activity of the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>pre-frontal cortex</b></span> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreased</i></span>&nbsp;- causing <u>non-sensical dreams</u>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Substance use</b></span>&nbsp;<b style="""">disorders&nbsp;</b>are social changes induced by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">drug use</span> that alter <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">work &amp; family life</span>.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Methadone&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[drug]</span> is used as a treatment for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>heroin</u></span> useage since it acts as an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>agonist</i></span> at <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">opiate</span> receptors."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Methadone</b></span> is used as a treatment for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>heroin</u></span> useage since it acts as an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>agonist</i></span> at <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">opiate</span> receptors.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Explains the <b><u>cocktail party effect</u></b> - we're primed to respond to our names.</div><div><br></div><img src=""95434-91979.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Priming</b></span> is when exposure to 1 stimulus <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;unconsciously&#x20;influences&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> our response to <i>another </i>stimulus."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Priming</b></span> is when exposure to 1 stimulus <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>unconsciously influences</i></span> our response to <i>another </i>stimulus.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Explains the <b><u>cocktail party effect</u></b> - we're primed to respond to our names.</div><div><br></div><img src=""95434-91979.jpg""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;resource&#x20;model&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of attention says we can get <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>overwhelmed</i></span> if we try to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">

focus on&nbsp;<i>too much</i></span> at once."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>resource model</b></span> of attention says we can get <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>overwhelmed</i></span> if we try to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">

focus on&nbsp;<i>too much</i></span> at once.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Says processing comes&nbsp;<i>before&nbsp;</i>it decides what to filter out.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""deutsch-deutsch-late-selection-theory-l.jpg""></div></div>"

"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">primacy</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">recency</span> effects are <i>collectively</i> called the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;serial&#x20;position&#x20;effect&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>." "In <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>working</i></span> memory, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>central executive</b></span> combines information from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">visiospatial sketchpad</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">phonological loop</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>working</i></span> memory, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>central executive</b></span> combines information from the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""visiospatial&#x20;sketchpad"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""phonological&#x20;loop"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>working</i></span> memory, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>central executive</b></span> combines information from the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">visiospatial sketchpad</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">phonological loop</span>.

<i>1</i> sounds like <i>gun</i>.</div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;state&#x2D;dependent&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> retrieval cue relies on you being in the same <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">""<i>headspace""</i></span> to remember information." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Failing to recognize something is <i>really</i> there.<div><br></div><div><img src=""b2a0981fcecb48b2d158571e27561c52.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;availability&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>heuristic</b> makes decisions by using <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">anectdotal examples</span> <i>fresh</i> in our minds."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>availability</b></span> <b>heuristic</b> makes decisions by using <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">anectdotal examples</span> <i>fresh</i> in our minds.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- ""Shark attacks are more common than firework accidents... I just heard about a shark attack on the news!""<div>- Representativeness heuristic is when we expect a person likes Z just because they like X &amp; Y</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-26502456eac0f4998043eb794d588b105ffcc156.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>availability</b></span> <b>heuristic</b> makes decisions by using <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""anectdotal&#x20;examples"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <i>fresh</i> in our minds."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>availability</b></span> <b>heuristic</b> makes decisions by using <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">anectdotal examples</span> <i>fresh</i> in our minds.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- ""Shark attacks are more common than firework accidents... I just heard about a shark attack on the news!""<div>- Representativeness heuristic is when we expect a person likes Z just because they like X &amp; Y</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-26502456eac0f4998043eb794d588b105ffcc156.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;representativeness&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>heuristic</b>&nbsp;makes judgements about something based on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">what's <i>typical&nbsp;</i>for it to do</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>representativeness</b></span> <b>heuristic</b>&nbsp;makes judgements about something based on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">what's <i>typical&nbsp;</i>for it to do</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Ex: ""She's a feminist, so I bet she voted for Bernie too!""<div>- <u>Base rate fallacy</u> &amp; <u>conjunction fallacy</u> are both sub-classes of the <b>representativeness heuristic</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-26502456eac0f4998043eb794d588b105ffcc156.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>representativeness</b></span> <b>heuristic</b>&nbsp;makes judgements about something based on <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""what&#x27;s&#x20;&lt;i&gt;typical&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;for&#x20;it&#x20;to&#x20;do"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>representativeness</b></span> <b>heuristic</b>&nbsp;makes judgements about something based on <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">what's <i>typical&nbsp;</i>for it to do</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Ex: ""She's a feminist, so I bet she voted for Bernie too!""<div>- <u>Base rate fallacy</u> &amp; <u>conjunction fallacy</u> are both sub-classes of the <b>representativeness heuristic</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-26502456eac0f4998043eb794d588b105ffcc156.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;u&gt;conjunction&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;<b>fallacy</b>&nbsp;is when we assume just because a person believes X, they must <i>also </i>believe Y &amp; Z."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><u>conjunction</u></b></span>&nbsp;<b>fallacy</b>&nbsp;is when we assume just because a person believes X, they must <i>also </i>believe Y &amp; Z.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

""It's more likely that she's a feminist <i>and </i>a Bernie surrporter than just a feminist""</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Belief&#x20;perserverance&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;is when we <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>ignore&nbsp;</i>facts&nbsp;<i>counter</i>&nbsp;to our own</span> to <i>maintain</i>&nbsp;current beliefs."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Belief perserverance</b></span>&nbsp;is when we <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>ignore&nbsp;</i>facts&nbsp;<i>counter</i>&nbsp;to our own</span> to <i>maintain</i>&nbsp;current beliefs.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Think of people doing this with politicians.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Belief perserverance</b></span>&nbsp;is when we <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;ignore&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;facts&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;counter&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;to&#x20;our&#x20;own"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to <i>maintain</i>&nbsp;current beliefs." Think of people doing this with politicians.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Confirmation&#x20;bias&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is our tendency to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>favor</i> information</span> that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>confirms our pre-existing beliefs</u></span> - occuring the most with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">emotionally-charged</span> topics."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Confirmation bias</b></span> is our tendency to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>favor</i> information</span> that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>confirms our pre-existing beliefs</u></span> - occuring the most with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">emotionally-charged</span> topics.

</div>"

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Confirmation bias</b></span> is our tendency to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>favor</i> information</span> that <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>confirms our pre-existing beliefs</u></span> - occuring the most with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">emotionally-charged</span> topics.

</div>"

"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>framing effect</b></span> describes how bias can be induced by the <i>tone </i>of how you ask a question.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Binet</span>'s idea of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">mental age</b></span> refers to how a child of a given age <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;performs&#x20;intellectually&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> to <i>other</i> children that age."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Binet</span>'s idea of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">mental age</b></span> refers to how a child of a given age <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u style="""">performs intellectually</u></span> to <i>other</i> children that age.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Conduction aphasia</b></span> is characterized by<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>difficulty&nbsp;</i><u>repeating&nbsp;words</u></span> given to you by other people.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>You can understand most things &amp; speak clearly, but you have a hard time responding to instructions.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Arcuate-fasciculus (1).jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><a href=""https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Mq_SuaqW22M"">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Mq_SuaqW22M</a><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Conduction aphasia</b></span> is characterized by<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;difficulty&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;repeating&amp;nbsp&#x3B;words&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> given to you by other people."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Conduction aphasia</b></span> is characterized by<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>difficulty&nbsp;</i><u>repeating&nbsp;words</u></span> given to you by other people.

Children immitate adults&nbsp;→&nbsp;receive rewards<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-6618633ba1ae0c1302f471e428b78c5bcbfffee2.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Nativists&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[nativists/behaviorists/interactionists]</span> believe language is&nbsp;<u style="""">innate</u>&nbsp;to humans."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Nativists</b></span> believe language is&nbsp;<u style="""">innate</u>&nbsp;to humans.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Heavy emphasis on the importance of the critical period on language development.</div><div>- Noam Chomsky is a nativist.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-6618633ba1ae0c1302f471e428b78c5bcbfffee2.jpg""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Linguistic&#x20;determinism&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[language theory]</span> says that our <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>language</u></span> <i>completely controls</i>&nbsp;our <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>cognition</u></span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Linguistic determinism</b></span> says that our <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>language</u></span> <i>completely controls</i>&nbsp;our <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>cognition</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Believed that - without the ability to think - we wouldn't have anything to describe through language (i.e. cognition controls language).</div><div>- They attribute this to the fact that they believe all langauges share common attributes (like nouns).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-3bdf9fa26a2dced1078a7bf11b986c6d87b15e0c.jpg""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Linguistic determinism</b></span> says that our <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;language&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <i>completely controls</i>&nbsp;our <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;cognition&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Linguistic determinism</b></span> says that our <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>language</u></span> <i>completely controls</i>&nbsp;our <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>cognition</u></span>.</div>

<div>He also believed language &amp; cognitive abilities developed separately in children, but later converged together.</div><div><br></div><img src=""Lev_Vygotsky_1896-1934.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-3d423fe27aae0d2fdcaa9b9e26544c1995d7a6ab.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Linguistic&#x20;determinism&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the idea that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>language</u>&nbsp;<i>completely determines</i>&nbsp;our <u>cognition</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Linguistic determinism</b></span> is the idea that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>language</u>&nbsp;<i>completely determines</i>&nbsp;our <u>cognition</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Weak &amp; strong linguistic determinism.<div><br></div><div>- Weak: linguistic relativism</div><div>- Strong: Sapir-Worfian hypothesis</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Linguistic determinism</b></span> is the idea that <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;language&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;completely&#x20;determines&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;our&#x20;&lt;u&gt;cognition&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Linguistic determinism</b></span> is the idea that <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>language</u>&nbsp;<i>completely determines</i>&nbsp;our <u>cognition</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Weak &amp; strong linguistic determinism.<div><br></div><div>- Weak: linguistic relativism</div><div>- Strong: Sapir-Worfian hypothesis</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Strong"" data-ordinal=""1"">[weak/strong]</span> <b>linguistic determinism </b>says&nbsp;<u>language</u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>completely determines</i></span> our <u>thoughts</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Strong</span> <b>linguistic determinism </b>says&nbsp;<u>language</u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>completely determines</i></span> our <u>thoughts</u>.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Linguistic&#x20;relativism&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> says that differences <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>do</i></span>&nbsp;exist between <u>different languages</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Linguistic relativism</b></span> says that differences <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>do</i></span>&nbsp;exist between <u>different languages</u>.

Also believes that cognition can influence language development without entirely controlling it.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-3d423fe27aae0d2fdcaa9b9e26544c1995d7a6ab.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Sapir&#x2D;Whorfian&#x20;hypothesis&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the theory that says <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>language</u>&nbsp;either <i>influences</i> or <i>completely determines</i> <u>cognition</u></span> - encompassing linguistic relativism and linguistic determinism.</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Sapir-Whorfian hypothesis</b></span> is the theory that says <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>language</u>&nbsp;either <i>influences</i> or <i>completely determines</i> <u>cognition</u></span> - encompassing linguistic relativism and linguistic determinism.</div>

"What's the <i>difference </i>between the <b>behaviorist </b>&amp; <b>interactionist </b>theory of <u><i>language </i>development</u>?<div><br></div><div><b>Behaviorist</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""language&#x20;develops&#x20;from&#x20;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;operant&#x20;conditioning&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&#x28;giving&#x20;&quot;rewards&quot;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div><b>Interactionist</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""language&#x20;develops&#x20;from&#x20;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;wanting&#x20;to&#x20;communicate&#x20;with&#x20;adults&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"What's the <i>difference </i>between the <b>behaviorist </b>&amp; <b>interactionist </b>theory of <u><i>language </i>development</u>?<div><br></div><div><b>Behaviorist</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">language develops from <i><u>operant conditioning</u></i> (giving ""rewards"")</span></div><div><b>Interactionist</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">language develops from <i><u>wanting to communicate with adults</u></i></span></div>

"What's the <i>difference </i>between the <b>behaviorist </b>&amp; <b>interactionist </b>theory of <u><i>language </i>development</u>?<div><br></div><div><b>Interactionist</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""language&#x20;develops&#x20;from&#x20;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;wanting&#x20;to&#x20;communicate&#x20;with&#x20;adults&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div><div><b>Behaviorist</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""language&#x20;develops&#x20;from&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;operant&#x20;conditioning&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&#x28;giving&#x20;&quot;rewards&quot;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"What's the <i>difference </i>between the <b>behaviorist </b>&amp; <b>interactionist </b>theory of <u><i>language </i>development</u>?<div><br></div><div><b>Interactionist</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">language develops from <i><u>wanting to communicate with adults</u></i></span><br></div><div><b>Behaviorist</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">language develops from&nbsp;<i><u>operant conditioning</u></i>&nbsp;(giving ""rewards"")</span><br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-6618633ba1ae0c1302f471e428b78c5bcbfffee2.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Vygotsky&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[person]</span> falls into the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>interactionist</u></span> group of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">language</span> development." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Interactionist</b>: children develop language in order to communicate with adults.<div><div>- Vygotsky personally believed that cognition and language developed separately, but later emerge together.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-3d423fe27aae0d2fdcaa9b9e26544c1995d7a6ab.jpg""><br></div></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Vygotsky</b></span> falls into the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;interactionist&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[natavist/behaviorist/interactionist]</span> group of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">language</span> development."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Vygotsky</b></span> falls into the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>interactionist</u></span> group of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">language</span> development.
<hr>

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Vygotsky</b></span> falls into the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>interactionist</u></span> group of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""language"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> development." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Encoding a memory with <i>mild </i>fear&nbsp;→ <i style="""">optimal </i>memory</div><div>Encoding a memory with <i>extreme </i>fear&nbsp;→ <i>bad </i>memory (think trauma)</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2000px-HebbianYerkesDodson.svg.png""><br></div></div>"
"Our ability to remember <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>long</i></span>-term memories&nbsp;encoded with feelings of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>fear</u></span> follow the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Yerkes&#x20;Dodson&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><b> Law.</b>"	"Our ability to remember <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>long</i></span>-term memories&nbsp;encoded with feelings of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>fear</u></span> follow the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Yerkes Dodson</b></span><b> Law.</b>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Encoding a memory with <i>mild </i>fear&nbsp;→ <i style="""">optimal </i>memory</div><div>Encoding a memory with <i>extreme </i>fear&nbsp;→ <i>bad </i>memory (think trauma)</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2000px-HebbianYerkesDodson.svg.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Richard Lazarus</u></span> was the major proponent behind the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;u&gt;appraisal&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b> of stress</b>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Richard Lazarus</u></span> was the major proponent behind the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><u>appraisal theory</u></b></span><b> of stress</b>.

"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>reticular formation</b></span> in the brainstem controls our <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>awareness</i></u></span> &amp; <i>transmits</i>&nbsp;information to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>thalamus</u></span>.

"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>inhibitory</i></span> neurotransmitters are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;GABA&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> (main) &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;glycine&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><i>- </i>Apart of the <b><u>tuberoinfundibular pathway</u></b> (arcuate nucleus → posterior pituitary).</div><div>- Not the only place where dopamine is produced.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1200px-Tuberoinfundibular_pathway.svg.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;tuberoinfundibular&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>pathway</b> sends <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>dopamine</i></span> from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>arcuate&nbsp;</u><u>nucleus</u></span> → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>median eminence</u></span> to regulate prolactin levels." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>EEGs: only shows <i>general</i> brain activity</div><div>fMRIs: shows brain activity &amp; <i>specific </i>regions where it's occurring.</div><div><br></div><img src=""eeg-vs-fmri-e1521126726262.png""></div>"
"What's the <i>difference </i>between <b>EEGs </b>&amp; <b>fMRIs</b>?<div><br></div><div><b>EEGs</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""detects&#x20;&lt;u&gt;electrical&#x20;activity&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;in&#x20;the&#x20;brain&#x20;&#x28;&lt;i&gt;doesn&#x27;t&#x20;tell&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;regions&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div><b>fMRIs</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""detects&#x20;&lt;u&gt;blood&#x20;flow&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;to&#x20;&lt;i&gt;specific&amp;nbsp&#x3B;regions&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;in&#x20;the&#x20;brain&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"What's the <i>difference </i>between <b>EEGs </b>&amp; <b>fMRIs</b>?<div><br></div><div><b>EEGs</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">detects <u>electrical activity</u> in the brain (<i>doesn't tell&nbsp;</i>regions)</span></div><div><b>fMRIs</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">detects <u>blood flow</u> to <i>specific&nbsp;regions </i>in the brain.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""eeg-vs-fmri-e1521126726262.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>fMRIs</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>detect brain activity by mapping <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;blood&#x20;flow&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> to <i>specific </i>regions in the brain."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>fMRIs</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>detect brain activity by mapping <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>blood flow</u></span> to <i>specific </i>regions in the brain.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Tells you <i>both </i>the function &amp; structure - i.e. tells you <i>what's </i>happening &amp; <i>where </i>it's happening.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""eeg-vs-fmri-e1521126726262.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;fMRIs&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><b>&nbsp;</b>detect brain activity by mapping <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>blood flow</u></span> to <i>specific </i>regions in the brain."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>fMRIs</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>detect brain activity by mapping <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>blood flow</u></span> to <i>specific </i>regions in the brain.

"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>drive-reduction theory</b></span><i style="""">&nbsp;</i>believes our <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>physiological&nbsp;needs</u></span> <i>drive </i>our <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>behaviors</u></span> until<i> </i>the need is satisfied.

- Emphasis on <b>external </b>factors controlling behavior.</div>"

- Emphasis on <b>external </b>factors controlling behavior.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Situational&#x20;attribution&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is how we attribute the actions of others to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>external</i> factors</span> as opposed to blaming their character."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Situational attribution</b></span> is how we attribute the actions of others to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>external</i> factors</span> as opposed to blaming their character.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Situational attribution 3 components: consensus, consistency, distinctiveness<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""1200-563866-situational-vs-dispositional.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""de19db609180f2ff76fc386290a9ee1c.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Situational attribution</b></span> is how we attribute the actions of others to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;external&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;factors"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> as opposed to blaming their character."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Situational attribution</b></span> is how we attribute the actions of others to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>external</i> factors</span> as opposed to blaming their character.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Situational attribution 3 components: consensus, consistency, distinctiveness<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""1200-563866-situational-vs-dispositional.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""de19db609180f2ff76fc386290a9ee1c.png""><br></div></div>"
"In <b>situational attribution</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;consistency&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is whether or not a person <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>regularly </i>behaves that way</span>."	"In <b>situational attribution</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>consistency</u></span> is whether or not a person <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>regularly </i>behaves that way</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"In <b>situational attribution</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>consistency</u></span> is whether or not a person <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>regularly </i>behaves that way</span>.

<div><div>- She's&nbsp;<i>always&nbsp;</i>late with her assignments!</div></div><div>- Situational attribution 3 components: consensus, consistency, distinctiveness<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""1200-563866-situational-vs-dispositional.jpg""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- She's only ever late with her assignments when Jenny sits next to her...</div><div>- Situational attribution 3 components: consensus, consistency, distinctiveness</div><div><br></div><img src=""1200-563866-situational-vs-dispositional.jpg""></div>"
"In <b>attribution theory</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>distinctiveness</u></span> is whether or not a person <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""acts&#x20;&lt;i&gt;differently&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in <i>different </i>social situations."	"In <b>attribution theory</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>distinctiveness</u></span> is whether or not a person <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">acts <i>differently</i></span> in <i>different </i>social situations.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- She's only ever late with her assignments when Jenny sits next to her...</div><div>- Situational attribution 3 components: consensus, consistency, distinctiveness</div><div><br></div><img src=""1200-563866-situational-vs-dispositional.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In&nbsp;<b>attribution theory</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;consensus&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when a person acts the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>same&nbsp;</i>way as everyone else</span> in the same situation.</div>"	"<div>In&nbsp;<b>attribution theory</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>consensus</u></span> is when a person acts the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>same&nbsp;</i>way as everyone else</span> in the same situation.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>- Everyone seemed to have gotten number 3 wrong... maybe I worded it confusingly?</div></div><div>- Attribution theory 3 components: <b><u>consensus</u></b>, <b><u>consistency</u></b>, <u style=""font-weight: bold;"">distinctiveness</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""1200-563866-situational-vs-dispositional.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In&nbsp;<b>attribution theory</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>consensus</u></span> is when a person acts the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;same&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;way&#x20;as&#x20;everyone&#x20;else"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in the same situation.</div>"	"<div>In&nbsp;<b>attribution theory</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>consensus</u></span> is when a person acts the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>same&nbsp;</i>way as everyone else</span> in the same situation.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Apart of the humanistic theory, developed by <b>Carl Rogers</b></div><div><br></div><img src=""220px-Carlrogers.jpg""></div>"
"Freud's <b>psychoanalytic theory</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;deterministic&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[deterministic/indeterministic]</span>, since it says our behavior is&nbsp;<i>determined</i> <i>entirely </i>by our unconscious."	"Freud's <b>psychoanalytic theory</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">deterministic</u></span>, since it says our behavior is&nbsp;<i>determined</i> <i>entirely </i>by our unconscious.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""d0e36082c2eb9b7cd13030271707b221.png""></div>"
"Whereas <b>psychoanalytic</b> <b>theory </b>focuses on the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;unconscious&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> mind, <b>humanistic theory</b>&nbsp;focuses on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i><u>conscious</u></i></span> mind."	"Whereas <b>psychoanalytic</b> <b>theory </b>focuses on the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>unconscious</i></span> mind, <b>humanistic theory</b>&nbsp;focuses on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i><u>conscious</u></i></span> mind.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Humanistic theory developed by <b>Carl Rogers</b>.<div><br></div><div><img src=""d0e36082c2eb9b7cd13030271707b221.png""><br></div></div>"
"Whereas <b>psychoanalytic</b> <b>theory </b>focuses on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>unconscious</i></span> mind, <b>humanistic theory</b>&nbsp;focuses on the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;conscious&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> mind."	"Whereas <b>psychoanalytic</b> <b>theory </b>focuses on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>unconscious</i></span> mind, <b>humanistic theory</b>&nbsp;focuses on the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i><u>conscious</u></i></span> mind.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Humanistic theory developed by <b>Carl Rogers</b>.<div><br></div><div><img src=""d0e36082c2eb9b7cd13030271707b221.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Maslow's hierarchy</b> of needs was developed under the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;humanistic&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span> of <u>personality</u>."	"<b>Maslow's hierarchy</b> of needs was developed under the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>humanistic theory</b></span> of <u>personality</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Humanistic: ""people strive towards self-actualization""</div><div><br></div><img src=""1200px-MaslowsHierarchyOfNeeds.svg.png""></div>"
"<i>Less </i>than <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>% of people truly achieve <b>self-actualization</b>."	"<i>Less </i>than <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>% of people truly achieve <b>self-actualization</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""1200px-MaslowsHierarchyOfNeeds.svg.png""></div>"
"The humanistic theory believes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>self-actualization</b></span> can&nbsp;<i>only</i> be reached when a person is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;genuine&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; feels <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;acceptance&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> from others."	"The humanistic theory believes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>self-actualization</b></span> can&nbsp;<i>only</i> be reached when a person is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>genuine</u></span> &amp; feels <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>acceptance</u></span> from others.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Genuine</b>: you're open about yourself and your own flaws<div><div>- <b>Acceptance</b>: others accept you for who you are.</div></div></div>"
"The humanistic theory believes <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;self&#x2D;actualization&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> can&nbsp;<i>only</i> be reached when a person is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>genuine</u></span> &amp; feels <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>acceptance</u></span> from others."	"The humanistic theory believes <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>self-actualization</b></span> can&nbsp;<i>only</i> be reached when a person is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>genuine</u></span> &amp; feels <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>acceptance</u></span> from others.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Genuine</b>: you're open about yourself and your own flaws<div><div>- <b>Acceptance</b>: others accept you for who you are.</div></div></div>"
"According to Carl Rogers, being <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;genuine&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> means to be <i>honest </i>with yourself and your own flaws."	"According to Carl Rogers, being <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>genuine</b></span> means to be <i>honest </i>with yourself and your own flaws.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Being <u>genuine</u><b> </b>&amp; <u>feeling accepted</u> are necessary to achieve self-actualization.</div>"
"The idea of the <b>self-concept</b> belongs to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""humanistic&#x20;theory"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span> of <u>personality</u>."	"The idea of the <b>self-concept</b> belongs to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">humanistic theory</span> of <u>personality</u>.

- <b>Hans Eysenck</b><div>- <b>Jeffrey Alan Gray</b></div><div>- <b>C. Robert Cloninger</b></div><div><b><br></b></div><div><img src=""d0e36082c2eb9b7cd13030271707b221.png""><b><br></b></div></div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- He believed that introverts &amp; extroverts differed by their reticular formation development.</div><div><br></div>- Theory that our personality is derived largely by our <b>genes</b>.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Jeffrey Alan Gray</span>&nbsp;developed the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;biopsychosocial&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span> of <u>personality</u>."
