<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Falls under the <b><u>biological theory</u></b> of personality.</div><div><br></div>- Theory that our personality is derived largely by our <b>genes</b>.<div><br></div><div><img src=""d0e36082c2eb9b7cd13030271707b221.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>C. Robert Cloninger</b>&nbsp;believes in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""biological&#x20;theory"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span> of <u>personality</u>."	"<b>C. Robert Cloninger</b>&nbsp;believes in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">biological theory</span> of <u>personality</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Biological theory: our personality is derived largely by our <b>genes</b>.<div>- Cloninger believes our brain regions/neurotransmitters determine behavior.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-17587f30c03b213d8f3c43ac4ab02a4281672bf3.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Hans&#x20;Eysenck&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[person]</span> believed that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">introverts</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">extroverts</span> have <i>differently</i> developed <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>reticular formations</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hans Eysenck</b></span> believed that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">introverts</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">extroverts</span> have <i>differently</i> developed <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>reticular formations</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>Biological theory</b> of personality.<div>- Reticular formation is responsible for regulating our <b>arousal</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""screen_shot_2016-03-07_at_5.54.01_pm_116998.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hans Eysenck</b></span> believed that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">introverts</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">extroverts</span> have <i>differently</i> developed <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;reticular&#x20;formations&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hans Eysenck</b></span> believed that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">introverts</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">extroverts</span> have <i>differently</i> developed <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>reticular formations</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>Biological theory</b> of personality.<div>- Reticular formation is responsible for regulating our <b>arousal</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""screen_shot_2016-03-07_at_5.54.01_pm_116998.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hans Eysenck</b></span> believed that <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""introverts"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""extroverts"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> have <i>differently</i> developed <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>reticular formations</u></span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>Biological theory</b> of personality.<div>- Reticular formation is responsible for regulating our <b>arousal</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""screen_shot_2016-03-07_at_5.54.01_pm_116998.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Hans&#x20;Eysenck&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[person]</span> believed <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">introverts</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i style="""">more</i></span> naturally aroused due to having&nbsp;<i style="""">better&nbsp;</i>developed <u>reticular formations</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Hans Eysenck</b></span> believed <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">introverts</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i style="""">more</i></span> naturally aroused due to having&nbsp;<i style="""">better&nbsp;</i>developed <u>reticular formations</u>.

"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>biopsychosocial model</b></span> believes our <u>personality</u> is controlled by behavioral <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;activating&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;inhibiting&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> systems."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>biopsychosocial model</b></span> believes our <u>personality</u> is controlled by behavioral <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>activating</i></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>inhibiting</i></span> systems.

"<u>Gordon Allport</u> vs. <u>Hans Eysenck</u> on <b>personality <i>traits</i></b>...<div><br></div><div><b>Allport</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""we&#x20;each&#x20;posess&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;u&gt;only&#x20;certain&#x20;traits&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div><b>Eysenck</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""we&#x20;&lt;u&gt;posess&#x20;all&#x20;traits&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;but&#x20;only&#x20;express&#x20;them&#x20;in&#x20;different&#x20;amounts"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<u>Gordon Allport</u> vs. <u>Hans Eysenck</u> on <b>personality <i>traits</i></b>...<div><br></div><div><b>Allport</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">we each posess&nbsp;<u>only certain traits</u></span></div><div><b>Eysenck</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">we <u>posess all traits</u> but only express them in different amounts</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Gorodon Allport: founded trait theory of behavior.</div><div><br></div>- Hans Eysenck also believed the difference between <u>introverts</u> &amp; <u>extroverts </u>comes from their <b>reticular formations</b>.</div>"
"<u>Gordon Allport</u> vs. <u>Hans Eysenck</u> on <b>personality <i>traits</i></b>...<div><br></div><div><b>Eysenck</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""we&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;u&gt;posess&#x20;all&#x20;traits&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;but&#x20;only&#x20;express&#x20;them&#x20;in&#x20;different&#x20;amounts"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.<b><br></b></div><div><b>Allport</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""we&#x20;each&#x20;posess&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;u&gt;only&#x20;&lt;i&gt;certain&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;traits&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<u>Gordon Allport</u> vs. <u>Hans Eysenck</u> on <b>personality <i>traits</i></b>...<div><br></div><div><b>Eysenck</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">we&nbsp;<u>posess all traits</u>&nbsp;but only express them in different amounts</span>.<b><br></b></div><div><b>Allport</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">we each posess&nbsp;<u>only <i>certain </i>traits</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>- Gorodon Allport: founded trait theory of behavior.</div><div><br></div>- Hans Eysenck also believed the difference between&nbsp;<u>introverts</u>&nbsp;&amp;&nbsp;<u>extroverts&nbsp;</u>comes from their&nbsp;<b>reticular formations</b>.</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Hans Eysenck</u></span> believed we <i>each </i>have 3 major categories of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">personality traits</span>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;psychoticism&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;extroversion&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;neuroticism&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> expressed in different degrees."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Hans Eysenck</u></span> believed we <i>each </i>have 3 major categories of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">personality traits</span>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>psychoticism</b></span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>extroversion</b></span>, &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>neuroticism</b></span> expressed in different degrees.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Mirror&#x20;neurons&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b>&nbsp;</b>fire <i>both </i>when we <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">perform</span> an action&nbsp;&amp; when we <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">observe</span> that <i>same action</i> from someone else."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mirror neurons</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>fire <i>both </i>when we <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">perform</span> an action&nbsp;&amp; when we <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">observe</span> that <i>same action</i> from someone else.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Tricks our mind into thing we performed the action ourselves... <u>observational learning</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""gettyimages-1080455448-68ce10d3395e2c6d73c17666ad90b28140674071.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mirror neurons</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b>fire <i>both </i>when we <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""perform"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> an action&nbsp;&amp; when we <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""observe"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> that <i>same action</i> from someone else." "Aggression is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;learned&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[innate/learned]</span> behavior."	"Aggression is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>learned</i></u></span> behavior.

Bobo doll experiment.</div>"

<div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Alogia</b>&nbsp;→ bad in conversations</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Avolition</b>&nbsp;→ lacking goals.</div></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""what-are-the-symptoms-of-schizophrenia-2953120-cba74c5e1dd942ecafde1824217603f9.png""><br></div></div>"
"In the&nbsp;<u>negative</u>&nbsp;symptoms of schizophrenia, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;alogia&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when individuals become&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>unable&nbsp;</i>to hold conversations</span>." <div>-&nbsp;<u>Type</u>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<b>dissociative disorder</b>, which is when individuals have disruptions in their memory &amp; identity.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""800.jpg""></div>"
"<i>Why </i>do people usually develop <b>dissociate identity disorder</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""In&#x20;response&#x20;to&#x20;extreme&#x20;stress&#x20;&#x28;usually&#x20;during&#x20;childhood&#x29;&#x2E;&#x20;It&#x27;s&#x20;a&#x20;way&#x20;for&#x20;the&#x20;mind&#x20;to&#x20;cope&#x20;by&#x20;having&#x20;an&#x20;&quot;alter&#x20;ego&quot;&#x20;to&#x20;dissociate&#x20;emotions&#x20;from&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<i>Why </i>do people usually develop <b>dissociate identity disorder</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">In response to extreme stress (usually during childhood). It's a way for the mind to cope by having an ""alter ego"" to dissociate emotions from.</span></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These are disorders given to people with clear neurological problems despite doctors being unable to find underlying causes.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""85317f32eeed6a132ebc02dbb943f49f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Conversion-Disorder-statistics-FULL.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Conversion&#x20;disorder&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[disorder]</span> is when people experience<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>real&nbsp;</i><u>neurological symptoms</u></span> that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>cannot</i> be explained</span> by an underlying medical condition.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Conversion disorder</b></span> is when people experience<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>real&nbsp;</i><u>neurological symptoms</u></span> that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>cannot</i> be explained</span> by an underlying medical condition.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- A man's right arm is paralyzed but doctors can't find any underlying cause.</div><div>- Type of somatic symptom disorder.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""85317f32eeed6a132ebc02dbb943f49f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Conversion-Disorder-statistics-FULL.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Conversion disorder</b></span> is when people experience<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;real&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;neurological&#x20;symptoms&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> that <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;cannot&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;be&#x20;explained"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> by an underlying medical condition.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Conversion disorder</b></span> is when people experience<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>real&nbsp;</i><u>neurological symptoms</u></span> that <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>cannot</i> be explained</span> by an underlying medical condition.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Temporal lobe happens to be important for <b><u>memory</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Screen Shot 2019-03-23 at 1.19.21 PM.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>hippocampus</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>amygdala</u></span> are located in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;temporal&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>lobe</b>, which means the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""temporal"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> lobe is <i>crucial</i> for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">memory</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">emotional</span> processing.</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>hippocampus</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>amygdala</u></span> are located in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>temporal</b></span> <b>lobe</b>, which means the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">temporal</span> lobe is <i>crucial</i> for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">memory</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">emotional</span> processing.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...which is also the first area that starts to <u>degrade</u> in <b><u>Alzheimer's</u></b> patients.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Screen Shot 2019-03-23 at 1.19.21 PM.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Alzheimer&#x27;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;b&gt;s&#x20;disease&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[disease]</span> is associated with&nbsp;<i>accumulating </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>β-amyloid plaques</u></span> &amp; neurofibrillary <i>tangles</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Tau proteins</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Alzheimer'</b><b>s disease</b></span> is associated with&nbsp;<i>accumulating </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>β-amyloid plaques</u></span> &amp; neurofibrillary <i>tangles</i> of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Tau proteins</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b><u>Tau proteins</u></b> are responsible for regulating the stability of axons, which contributes to neural plasticity.</div><div><br></div><div>β-amyloid plaque + tau proteins = neural death (possibly).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Screen Shot 2019-03-23 at 1.19.21 PM.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Alzheimer'</b><b>s disease</b></span> is associated with&nbsp;<i>accumulating </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;β&#x2D;amyloid&#x20;plaques&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; neurofibrillary <i>tangles</i> of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;Tau&#x20;proteins&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Alzheimer'</b><b>s disease</b></span> is associated with&nbsp;<i>accumulating </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>β-amyloid plaques</u></span> &amp; neurofibrillary <i>tangles</i> of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Tau proteins</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b><u>Tau proteins</u></b> are responsible for regulating the stability of axons, which contributes to neural plasticity.</div><div><br></div><div>β-amyloid plaque + tau proteins = neural death (possibly).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Screen Shot 2019-03-23 at 1.19.21 PM.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Tau&#x20;proteins&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[proteins]</span> are <u>neural</u><i> </i>proteins that <i>stabilize </i>the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">microtubules of axons</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Tau proteins</b></span> are <u>neural</u><i> </i>proteins that <i>stabilize </i>the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">microtubules of axons</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These proteins tangle up in <b><u>Alzheimer's disease</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div>Important for regulating <b>neural plasticity</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Screen Shot 2019-03-23 at 1.19.21 PM.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Tau proteins</b></span> are <u>neural</u><i> </i>proteins that <i>stabilize </i>the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""microtubules&#x20;of&#x20;axons"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Tau proteins</b></span> are <u>neural</u><i> </i>proteins that <i>stabilize </i>the&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">microtubules of axons</span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These proteins tangle up in <b><u>Alzheimer's disease</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div>Important for regulating <b>neural plasticity</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Screen Shot 2019-03-23 at 1.19.21 PM.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In <b>Alzheimer's diease</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""neurofibrillary&#x20;tangles"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>collapsed&nbsp;</i><u>Tau proteins</u></span> that <i>interfere</i> with axonal stability.</div>"	"<div>In <b>Alzheimer's diease</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">neurofibrillary tangles</span> are<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>collapsed&nbsp;</i><u>Tau proteins</u></span> that <i>interfere</i> with axonal stability.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Important for regulating <b>neural plasticity</b>.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""shutterstock_114998065.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Screen Shot 2019-03-23 at 1.19.21 PM.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In <b>Alzheimer's diease</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">neurofibrillary tangles</span> are<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;collapsed&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;Tau&#x20;proteins&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> that <i>interfere</i> with axonal stability.</div>"	"<div>In <b>Alzheimer's diease</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">neurofibrillary tangles</span> are<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>collapsed&nbsp;</i><u>Tau proteins</u></span> that <i>interfere</i> with axonal stability.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Important for regulating <b>neural plasticity</b>.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""shutterstock_114998065.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Screen Shot 2019-03-23 at 1.19.21 PM.png""><br></div></div>"
"In the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;early&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[early/late]</span> stages of <b>Alzheimer's disease</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>basilis nuclei</u></span>&nbsp;<i>stop </i>releasing <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ACh</u></span> to the cortex - resulting in symptoms associated with a&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>loss&nbsp;</i>of motor control</span>."	"In the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">early</i></span> stages of <b>Alzheimer's disease</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>basilis nuclei</u></span>&nbsp;<i>stop </i>releasing <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ACh</u></span> to the cortex - resulting in symptoms associated with a&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>loss&nbsp;</i>of motor control</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">Septal nuclei</b> &amp; <b><u>basilis</u></b> are the main <u>cholinergic</u> neurons in the frontal lobe.</div><div>- Potentially related to the early loss of motor functioning.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Screen Shot 2019-03-23 at 1.19.21 PM.png""><br></div></div>"
"In the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">early</i></span> stages of <b>Alzheimer's disease</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;basilis&#x20;nuclei&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[nuclei]</span>&nbsp;<i>stop </i>releasing <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;ACh&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[neurotransmitter]</span> to the cortex - resulting in symptoms associated with a&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>loss&nbsp;</i>of motor control</span>."	"In the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">early</i></span> stages of <b>Alzheimer's disease</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>basilis nuclei</u></span>&nbsp;<i>stop </i>releasing <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ACh</u></span> to the cortex - resulting in symptoms associated with a&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""4""><i>loss&nbsp;</i>of motor control</span>.
<hr>

"The <u>genetic</u> basis behind <b>Alzheimer's disease</b> comes from mutations in genes that <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;metabolize&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;u&gt;amyloid&#x20;proteins&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <u>genetic</u> basis behind <b>Alzheimer's disease</b> comes from mutations in genes that <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>metabolize</i>&nbsp;<u>amyloid proteins</u></span>.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>Parkinson's disease</b></span> is caused by a destruction of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">dopami</span>nergic neurons in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>substantia nigra</u></span> within the basal ganglia.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Conformity"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the ""peer pressure"" that drives us to change our behavior to be&nbsp;<i>in line</i> with group norms."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Conformity</span> is the ""peer pressure"" that drives us to change our behavior to be&nbsp;<i>in line</i> with group norms.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""john-malkovich-e1435998981451.jpg""></div>"

"In conformity, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>informative influence</b></span> is when we assume <i>we're</i> wrong&nbsp;and that the <i>group </i>knows the right answer.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""john-malkovich-e1435998981451.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In conformity, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;normative&#x20;influence&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when we conform in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>be liked by others</u></span>.</div>"	"<div>In conformity, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>normative influence</b></span> is when we conform in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>be liked by others</u></span>.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Happens the most with individuals similar to each other.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-a93f62d215cbbe40704ab522b031326217c8cb53.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;span&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;Private&lt;&#x2F;span&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[public/private]</span> <b>conformity </b>is when we <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">change our&nbsp;<i>beliefs</i></span> to align with the group."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">Private</span></span> <b>conformity </b>is when we <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">change our&nbsp;<i>beliefs</i></span> to align with the group.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Public conformity: when we change our outward behavior to align with the group (maintaining original beliefs).</div><div>- Private conformity: changing our beliefs (we actually believe the group).</div><div><br></div><img src=""john-malkovich-e1435998981451.jpg""></div>"

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><span style=""font-weight: bold;"">Private</span></span> <b>conformity </b>is when we <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">change our&nbsp;<i>beliefs</i></span> to align with the group.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Group&#x20;polarization&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when the decision a group comes to is an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>amplified version</i></u></span> of their <i>original </i>opinion."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Group polarization</b></span> is when the decision a group comes to is an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>amplified version</i></u></span> of their <i>original </i>opinion.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Group polarization</b></span> is when the decision a group comes to is an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>amplified version</i></u></span> of their <i>original </i>opinion.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"Do 100% of the opinions of a group have to be the <i>same </i>for <b>group polarization</b> to occur?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;u&gt;No&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x2E;&#x20;The&#x20;group&#x20;instead&#x20;adopts&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;u&gt;most&#x20;popular&#x20;opinion&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;and&#x20;&lt;i&gt;amplifies&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;it&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Do 100% of the opinions of a group have to be the <i>same </i>for <b>group polarization</b> to occur?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><u>No</u></b>. The group instead adopts the <u>most popular opinion</u> and <i>amplifies</i> it.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b><u>Group polarization</u></b>: when groups reach a more amplified version of the original opinion as its consensus.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""john-malkovich-e1435998981451.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Groupthink&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when groups reach <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>irrational conclusions</u></span> due to its members <i>failing</i> to think critically on the issue because they care more about <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">maintaining group harmony</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Groupthink</b></span> is when groups reach <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>irrational conclusions</u></span> due to its members <i>failing</i> to think critically on the issue because they care more about <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">maintaining group harmony</span>.</div>

"What's the <i>difference </i>between the factors that comprise&nbsp;<b>social cognitive</b> theory &amp; <b>behavioral </b>theory?<div><br></div><div><b>Social cognitive</b>: we're shaped by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""our&#x20;&lt;u&gt;environment&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x2C;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;u&gt;perceptions&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;&lt;u&gt;behaviors&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><b>Behavioral</b>: we're shaped by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;only&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;by&#x20;our&#x20;&lt;u&gt;environment&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"What's the <i>difference </i>between the factors that comprise&nbsp;<b>social cognitive</b> theory &amp; <b>behavioral </b>theory?<div><br></div><div><b>Social cognitive</b>: we're shaped by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">our <u>environment</u>,&nbsp;<u>perceptions</u>, &amp; <u>behaviors</u></span>.</div><div><b>Behavioral</b>: we're shaped by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>only </i>by our <u>environment</u></span>.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Highest form of conformity.</div><div><br></div><img src=""01-types-of-conformity-power-point-7-638.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Gestalt&#x20;psychologists"" data-ordinal=""1"">[psychology group]</span> believe human behavior can <i>only </i>be studied as a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">whole</span>, rather than breaking it into smaller parts."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Gestalt psychologists</span> believe human behavior can <i>only </i>be studied as a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">whole</span>, rather than breaking it into smaller parts.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Gestalt psychology</b>: emphasized the idea that the ways in which our perceptual experiences are organized have to do with how our brains are organized.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-b1443c6690866f753d3adec42a4f24e247af76a1.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Gestalt psychologists</span> believe human behavior can <i>only </i>be studied as a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""whole"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>, rather than breaking it into smaller parts."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Gestalt psychologists</span> believe human behavior can <i>only </i>be studied as a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">whole</span>, rather than breaking it into smaller parts.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Gestalt psychology</b>: emphasized the idea that the ways in which our perceptual experiences are organized have to do with how our brains are organized.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-b1443c6690866f753d3adec42a4f24e247af76a1.jpg""></div>"
"<div><u>Conformity</u> experiment by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Solomon&#x20;Asch&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[person]</span>:</div><div><br></div><div>- 3 lines were drawn (labeled A, B, or C).</div><div>- Separate target line was drawn (similar in length to one of the 3 other lines).</div><div>- Participants were asked which line (A, B, or C) the target line <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">resembles in length</span>.</div><div>- Decoy participants gave <i>obviously</i> wrong answers.</div><div>- Did the real participant conform? Most of the time, yes.</div>"	"<div><u>Conformity</u> experiment by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Solomon Asch</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><div>- 3 lines were drawn (labeled A, B, or C).</div><div>- Separate target line was drawn (similar in length to one of the 3 other lines).</div><div>- Participants were asked which line (A, B, or C) the target line <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">resembles in length</span>.</div><div>- Decoy participants gave <i>obviously</i> wrong answers.</div><div>- Did the real participant conform? Most of the time, yes.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>If you're spent time preparing for a speech, being in the presence of other people will make you more likely to do it well. If you're underpreared, being around other people will make you more nervous.</div><div><br></div><img src=""social-facilitation.jpg""></div>"
"In <u>social facilitation</u>, our <b>dominant response</b> occurs in response to <i>increased </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""arousal&#x20;&#x28;nervous&#x20;energy&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> from being around other people."	"In <u>social facilitation</u>, our <b>dominant response</b> occurs in response to <i>increased </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">arousal (nervous energy)</span> from being around other people.

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b><u>Positive sanctions</u></b>: reward given for conforming to norms. ""You get an extra hour of TV for cleaning your room on your own!""</div><div>- <b><u>Negative sanctions</u></b>: punishment for violating norms (jail).</div><div>- <b><u>Informal sanctions</u></b>: unspoken rules that don't result in any direct rewards/punishments. <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These have no specific punishments.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6453593.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""folkways-mores-taboos-and-laws-3026267finalversion-5c8fe5a646e0fb000187a359.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Mores&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are social norms that are based in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>morality</u></span> &amp; are deemed <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>necessary for society</u></span> - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>minor</i></span> consequences when they're violated."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mores</b></span> are social norms that are based in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>morality</u></span> &amp; are deemed <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>necessary for society</u></span> - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>minor</i></span> consequences when they're violated.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><u>Going to the doctor</u> when you're sick or always <u>telling the truth</u> are examples of <b>mores</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""folkways-mores-taboos-and-laws-3026267finalversion-5c8fe5a646e0fb000187a359.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mores</b></span> are social norms that are based in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;morality&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; are deemed <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;necessary&#x20;for&#x20;society&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>minor</i></span> consequences when they're violated."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mores</b></span> are social norms that are based in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>morality</u></span> &amp; are deemed <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>necessary for society</u></span> - resulting in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>minor</i></span> consequences when they're violated.

"Violating <b>folkway norms</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> result in any <u>punishment</u>." "Violating <b>mores</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">does not</i></span> result in <i>severe</i> punishments.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"Violating social <b>taboos </b>results in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[more/less]</span> <u>severe consequences</u> than violating <b>mores</b>."	"Violating social <b>taboos </b>results in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>more</i></span> <u>severe consequences</u> than violating <b>mores</b>.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Taboos"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are behaviors <i>forbidden </i>by society that may or may not be against the law, like incest or cannibalism."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Taboos</span> are behaviors <i>forbidden </i>by society that may or may not be against the law, like incest or cannibalism.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""folkways-mores-taboos-and-laws-3026267finalversion-5c8fe5a646e0fb000187a359.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Deviance&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when individuals behave <i>outside</i>&nbsp;of what is acceptable by social norms."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Deviance</b></span> is when individuals behave <i>outside</i>&nbsp;of what is acceptable by social norms.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Deviance <b><u>isn't always negative</u></b>. Ex: most Americans eat meat, so <u>vegans</u> are deviants.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1486340150.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <b>extensor plantar response</b> is also called the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Babinski&#x20;reflex"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <b>extensor plantar response</b> is also called the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Babinski reflex</span>.

"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Hawthorne effect</b></span>&nbsp;is also called the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>observer effect</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Your behavior changes once you realize you're being watched.</div><div><br></div><img src=""1JX0tGZYix8iC2kXsPT6jKw.png""></div>"
"According to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;symbolic&#x20;interactionism&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[social theory]</span>, deviance can best be understood through everyday, <i>person-to-person</i>&nbsp;interactions."	"According to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>symbolic interactionism</b></span>, deviance can best be understood through everyday, <i>person-to-person</i>&nbsp;interactions.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>theory of differential association</b></span> says deviance is learned by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>surrounding yourself</i> with others</span>&nbsp;that regularly<i> </i><u>violate norms</u>.

"Learning <i>deviant </i>behavior from watching others supports the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">theory of differential association</span>.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Mass psychogenic illness</b></span>&nbsp;is a form of <u>mass hysteria</u> where people believe they all came down with the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">same illness</span> <i>despite </i>being <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">healthy</span>.

<div>- These people show <b><u>real symptoms</u></b>, induced by fear.</div><div>- After anthrax attacks, thousands of people believed they had the infection despite being healthy.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Dance_at_Molenbeek.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""-MhtRVV5GGM7bOwSKpBsByhf-SaCPwb0ar1W72jF42rZdC1-NPMiK2L0vYI6k7DJwgGLprFwT5-7aGkdOqT1SKM.png""><br></div></div>"

<div><img src=""a69a8dba-06bb-11ea-a958-5e9b7282cbd1.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""-MhtRVV5GGM7bOwSKpBsByhf-SaCPwb0ar1W72jF42rZdC1-NPMiK2L0vYI6k7DJwgGLprFwT5-7aGkdOqT1SKM.png""><br></div></div>"
"What's the <i>difference </i>between <b>mobs </b>&amp; <b>riots</b>?<div><br></div><div><b>Mobs</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""large&#x20;violent&#x20;crowd&#x20;targeting&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;specific&#x20;people&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&#x28;i&#x2E;e&#x2E;&#x20;dictators&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><b>Riots</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""large&#x20;violent&#x20;crowd&#x20;targeting&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;i&gt;general&#x20;system&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&#x28;i&#x2E;e&#x2E;&#x20;injustice&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"What's the <i>difference </i>between <b>mobs </b>&amp; <b>riots</b>?<div><br></div><div><b>Mobs</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">large violent crowd targeting&nbsp;<i>specific people</i> (i.e. dictators)</span>.</div><div><b>Riots</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">large violent crowd targeting the <i>general system</i> (i.e. injustice)</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""-MhtRVV5GGM7bOwSKpBsByhf-SaCPwb0ar1W72jF42rZdC1-NPMiK2L0vYI6k7DJwgGLprFwT5-7aGkdOqT1SKM.png""><br></div></div>"
"<u>Habituation</u> &amp; <u>sensitization</u> are types of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;non&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x2D;associative&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[associative/non-assocoative]</span> <b>learning</b>."	"<u>Habituation</u> &amp; <u>sensitization</u> are types of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>non</i>-associative</b></span> <b>learning</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

When you're repeatedly exposed to the same stimulus <u>without</u> ""connections"" between the two being shown.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7e1743ab786b03f5e6e58ec8ca9668e4beb276ef.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Habituation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is non-associative learning where we <i>slowly </i>become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">more used to a stimulus</span> &amp; tune it out."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Habituation</b></span> is non-associative learning where we <i>slowly </i>become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">more used to a stimulus</span> &amp; tune it out.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: slowly ""tuning out"" white noise over time.</div><div><br></div><img src=""white-noise.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7e1743ab786b03f5e6e58ec8ca9668e4beb276ef.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Habituation</b></span> is non-associative learning where we <i>slowly </i>become <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""more&#x20;used&#x20;to&#x20;a&#x20;stimulus"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; tune it out."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Habituation</b></span> is non-associative learning where we <i>slowly </i>become <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">more used to a stimulus</span> &amp; tune it out.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: slowly ""tuning out"" white noise over time.</div><div><br></div><img src=""white-noise.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7e1743ab786b03f5e6e58ec8ca9668e4beb276ef.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Sensitization&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is non-associative learning where we <i>slowly </i>become <i>more </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">frustrated</span> by a stimulus."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Sensitization</b></span> is non-associative learning where we <i>slowly </i>become <i>more </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">frustrated</span> by a stimulus.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""young-woman-yelling-holding-pillow-over-face-picture-id200188462-001.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7e1743ab786b03f5e6e58ec8ca9668e4beb276ef.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Sensitization</b></span> is non-associative learning where we <i>slowly </i>become <i>more </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""frustrated"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> by a stimulus."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Sensitization</b></span> is non-associative learning where we <i>slowly </i>become <i>more </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">frustrated</span> by a stimulus.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""young-woman-yelling-holding-pillow-over-face-picture-id200188462-001.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7e1743ab786b03f5e6e58ec8ca9668e4beb276ef.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Associative&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[associative/non-associative]</span> learning is when one stimulis is ""<i>connected</i>"" to another."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Associative</b></span> learning is when one stimulis is ""<i>connected</i>"" to another.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""2794861-classical-vs-operant-conditioning-5afc42a343a10300370da76f.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Classical</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">operant conditioning</span> are types of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;associative&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[associative/non-associative]</span> learning."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Classical</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">operant conditioning</span> are types of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>associative</i></b></span> learning.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><u>Habituation</u> &amp; <u>sensitization</u> are forms of non-associative learning.</div><div><br></div><img src=""2794861-classical-vs-operant-conditioning-5afc42a343a10300370da76f.png""></div>"
"<b>Classical conditioning</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> change the subject's <u>behavior</u>."	"<b>Classical conditioning</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">does not</i></span> change the subject's <u>behavior</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""></div>"
"In classical conditioning, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;unconditioned&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;<b>stimulus</b>&nbsp;is a stimulus you <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inherently </i>like or fear</span>, such as food or loud noises."	"In classical conditioning, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>unconditioned</b></span>&nbsp;<b>stimulus</b>&nbsp;is a stimulus you <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inherently </i>like or fear</span>, such as food or loud noises.

"In classical conditioning, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>unconditioned</b></span>&nbsp;<b>stimulus</b>&nbsp;is a stimulus you <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inherently </i>like or fear</span>, such as food or loud noises.

"In classical conditioning, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;unconditioned&#x20;response&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is a response you <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inherently </i>give</span>, such as salivating when you're near food."	"In classical conditioning, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>unconditioned response</b></span> is a response you <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inherently </i>give</span>, such as salivating when you're near food.

"In classical conditioning, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;neutral&#x20;stimulus&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is a stimulus that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">originally means <i>nothing&nbsp;</i>to you</span>, like a bell."	"In classical conditioning, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>neutral stimulus</b></span> is a stimulus that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">originally means <i>nothing&nbsp;</i>to you</span>, like a bell.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<b>Classical conditioning</b> pairs a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""neutral&#x20;stimulus&#x20;&#x28;bell&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> with an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""unconditioned&#x20;stimulus&#x20;&#x28;food&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> to produce a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>conditioned </i>response</span>."	"<b>Classical conditioning</b> pairs a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">neutral stimulus (bell)</span> with an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">unconditioned stimulus (food)</span> to produce a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>conditioned </i>response</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""></div>"
"<b>Classical conditioning</b> pairs a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">neutral stimulus (bell)</span> with an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">unconditioned stimulus (food)</span> to produce a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;conditioned&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;response"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<b>Classical conditioning</b> pairs a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">neutral stimulus (bell)</span> with an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">unconditioned stimulus (food)</span> to produce a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>conditioned </i>response</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""></div>"
"In <b>classical conditioning</b>, the <u><i>neutral </i>stimulus</u> becomes the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;conditioned&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;stimulus"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> after producing the conditioned response."	"In <b>classical conditioning</b>, the <u><i>neutral </i>stimulus</u> becomes the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>conditioned </i>stimulus</span> after producing the conditioned response.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""></div>"
"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;generalization&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when something <i>similar</i> to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>conditioned stimulus</u></span> gives the conditioned response."	"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>generalization</b></span> is when something <i>similar</i> to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>conditioned stimulus</u></span> gives the conditioned response.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: dog salivates when the doorbell is rang (instead of a handheld bell).</div><div><br></div><img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""></div>"
"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>generalization</b></span> is when something <i>similar</i> to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;conditioned&#x20;stimulus&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> gives the conditioned response."	"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>generalization</b></span> is when something <i>similar</i> to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>conditioned stimulus</u></span> gives the conditioned response.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: dog salivates when the doorbell is rang (instead of a handheld bell).</div><div><br></div><img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""></div>"
"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;discrimination&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when we can <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">tell the <i>difference</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>between the conditioned stimulus and something similar."	"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>discrimination</b></span> is when we can <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">tell the <i>difference</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>between the conditioned stimulus and something similar.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: dog doesn't salivate when the doorbell is rang - only when a handheld bell is rang.</div><div><br></div><img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""></div>"
"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>discrimination</b></span> is when we can <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""tell&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;i&gt;difference&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><i>&nbsp;</i>between the conditioned stimulus and something similar."	"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>discrimination</b></span> is when we can <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">tell the <i>difference</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>between the conditioned stimulus and something similar.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: dog doesn't salivate when the doorbell is rang - only when a handheld bell is rang.</div><div><br></div><img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""></div>"
"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;extinction&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> happens when you <i>lose </i>the conditioned response to the stimulus after time."	"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>extinction</b></span> happens when you <i>lose </i>the conditioned response to the stimulus after time.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Extinction can <u>only</u> occur by ending the reinforcement.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;spontaneous&#x20;recovery&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when you <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>randomly </i>regain</span> your <i>old </i>conditioning to a conditioned stimulus."	"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>spontaneous recovery</b></span> is when you <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>randomly </i>regain</span> your <i>old </i>conditioning to a conditioned stimulus.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: conditioning for the dog becomes extinct. Out of no where one day, the dog suddenly salivates when it hears the bell.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>spontaneous recovery</b></span> is when you <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;randomly&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;regain"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> your <i>old </i>conditioning to a conditioned stimulus."	"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>spontaneous recovery</b></span> is when you <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>randomly </i>regain</span> your <i>old </i>conditioning to a conditioned stimulus.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: conditioning for the dog becomes extinct. Out of no where one day, the dog suddenly salivates when it hears the bell.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;extinction&#x20;bursts&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>major </i>spikes in the conditioned response</span> just <i>before </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">conditioning becomes extinct</span>."	"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>extinction bursts</b></span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>major </i>spikes in the conditioned response</span> just <i>before </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">conditioning becomes extinct</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: dog salivates <u>a lot</u> right before its conditioning becomes extinct.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>extinction bursts</b></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;major&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;spikes&#x20;in&#x20;the&#x20;conditioned&#x20;response"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> just <i>before </i>the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""conditioning&#x20;becomes&#x20;extinct"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>extinction bursts</b></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>major </i>spikes in the conditioned response</span> just <i>before </i>the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">conditioning becomes extinct</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: dog salivates <u>a lot</u> right before its conditioning becomes extinct.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <b>extinction</b><b>&nbsp;</b>occurs when the animal <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[does/does not]</span> receive regular reinforcement."	"In <u>classical conditioning</u>, <b>extinction</b><b>&nbsp;</b>occurs when the animal <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>does not</i></span> receive regular reinforcement.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Aversive conditioning</b></span> is used in therapy to <i>pair </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;bad&#x20;habits&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;punishments&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> to help <i>break</i> the habit." "<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Implosive&#x20;therapy&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[conditioning]</span> is a form of <u>systematic desensitization</u> where the patient is <i>thrust into </i>the thing they fear most in hopes they over come it."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Implosive therapy</b></span> is a form of <u>systematic desensitization</u> where the patient is <i>thrust into </i>the thing they fear most in hopes they over come it.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""<b><u>Baptism by fire</u></b>""</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""fear-factor-3.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""12148314-0afb-42b7-9ff6-cc3be6dbe5c4.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Counterconditioning</b></span>&nbsp;pairs <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;fear&#x2D;inducing&#x20;stimuli&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;rewards&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in order to make us <i>enjoy</i> that stimulus."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Counterconditioning</b></span>&nbsp;pairs <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>fear-inducing stimuli</u></span> with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>rewards</u></span> in order to make us <i>enjoy</i> that stimulus.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""You were misbehaving, so you <u>can't go out</u> with your friends tonight.""</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c96dcb2c0d3f7c65973399284cceebed.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""2794863-operant-conditioning-a21-5b242abe8e1b6e0036fafff6-76a38a9afde76aa2baf449ea7caf24590488542a.png""></div>"
"In <u>operant conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;primary&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[1°/2°]</span>&nbsp;<b>reinforcers</b>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inherently </i>pleasurable rewards (food/sex)</span>."	"In <u>operant conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style=""font-weight: bold;"">primary</i></span>&nbsp;<b>reinforcers</b>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inherently </i>pleasurable rewards (food/sex)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""2794863-operant-conditioning-a21-5b242abe8e1b6e0036fafff6-76a38a9afde76aa2baf449ea7caf24590488542a.png""><br></div></div>"
"In <u>operant conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style=""font-weight: bold;"">primary</i></span>&nbsp;<b>reinforcers</b>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;inherently&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;pleasurable&#x20;rewards&#x20;&#x28;food&#x2F;sex&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"In <u>operant conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style=""font-weight: bold;"">primary</i></span>&nbsp;<b>reinforcers</b>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>inherently </i>pleasurable rewards (food/sex)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""2794863-operant-conditioning-a21-5b242abe8e1b6e0036fafff6-76a38a9afde76aa2baf449ea7caf24590488542a.png""><br></div></div>"
"In <u>operant conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;secondary&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;<b>reinforcers</b>&nbsp;are rewards we have to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>assign value</i>&nbsp;to (like money)</span>."	"In <u>operant conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>secondary</i></b></span>&nbsp;<b>reinforcers</b>&nbsp;are rewards we have to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>assign value</i>&nbsp;to (like money)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This is in contrast with primary reinforcers (<i>inherently </i>satisfying), like food or sex.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2794863-operant-conditioning-a21-5b242abe8e1b6e0036fafff6-76a38a9afde76aa2baf449ea7caf24590488542a.png""><br></div></div>"
"In <u>operant conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>secondary</i></b></span>&nbsp;<b>reinforcers</b>&nbsp;are rewards we have to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;assign&#x20;value&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;to&#x20;&#x28;like&#x20;money&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"In <u>operant conditioning</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>secondary</i></b></span>&nbsp;<b>reinforcers</b>&nbsp;are rewards we have to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>assign value</i>&nbsp;to (like money)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This is in contrast with primary reinforcers (<i>inherently </i>satisfying), like food or sex.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2794863-operant-conditioning-a21-5b242abe8e1b6e0036fafff6-76a38a9afde76aa2baf449ea7caf24590488542a.png""><br></div></div>"
"In operant conditioning, a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;token&#x20;economy&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when you're given <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>secondary</i></span> reinforcers (money) that you can later <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">exchange</span>&nbsp;for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>primary</i></span> reinforcers (food)."	"In operant conditioning, a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">token economy</b></span> is when you're given <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>secondary</i></span> reinforcers (money) that you can later <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">exchange</span>&nbsp;for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>primary</i></span> reinforcers (food).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""26295882-hand-handing-over-money-to-another-isolated-on-white.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2794863-operant-conditioning-a21-5b242abe8e1b6e0036fafff6-76a38a9afde76aa2baf449ea7caf24590488542a.png""><br></div></div>"
"In operant conditioning, a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">token economy</b></span> is when you're given <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;secondary&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> reinforcers (money) that you can later <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">exchange</span>&nbsp;for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;primary&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> reinforcers (food)."	"In operant conditioning, a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">token economy</b></span> is when you're given <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>secondary</i></span> reinforcers (money) that you can later <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">exchange</span>&nbsp;for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>primary</i></span> reinforcers (food).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""26295882-hand-handing-over-money-to-another-isolated-on-white.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2794863-operant-conditioning-a21-5b242abe8e1b6e0036fafff6-76a38a9afde76aa2baf449ea7caf24590488542a.png""><br></div></div>"
"In operant conditioning, a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">token economy</b></span> is when you're given <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>secondary</i></span> reinforcers (money) that you can later <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""exchange"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>&nbsp;for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>primary</i></span> reinforcers (food)."	"In operant conditioning, a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">token economy</b></span> is when you're given <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>secondary</i></span> reinforcers (money) that you can later <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">exchange</span>&nbsp;for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>primary</i></span> reinforcers (food).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""26295882-hand-handing-over-money-to-another-isolated-on-white.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2794863-operant-conditioning-a21-5b242abe8e1b6e0036fafff6-76a38a9afde76aa2baf449ea7caf24590488542a.png""><br></div></div>"
"In operant conditioning, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;operant&#x20;extinction&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> happens after people&nbsp;<u><i>stop </i>giving rewards</u>&nbsp;while <i>still </i>trying to reinforce behavior."	"In operant conditioning, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>operant extinction</b></span> happens after people&nbsp;<u><i>stop </i>giving rewards</u>&nbsp;while <i>still </i>trying to reinforce behavior.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: you keep telling your dog to sit but you stopped giving it treats. Eventually, it stops sitting.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2794863-operant-conditioning-a21-5b242abe8e1b6e0036fafff6-76a38a9afde76aa2baf449ea7caf24590488542a.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Instinctual&#x20;drift&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> occurs when animals&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>revert</i>&nbsp;to their&nbsp;<u>instinctual behaviors</u></span> after learning something through operant conditioning."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Instinctual drift</b></span> occurs when animals&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>revert</i>&nbsp;to their&nbsp;<u>instinctual behaviors</u></span> after learning something through operant conditioning.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: instead of racoons learning how to put coins in a piggy bank, they flip the coins around in their paws similar to what they do with food.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""2794863-operant-conditioning-a21-5b242abe8e1b6e0036fafff6-76a38a9afde76aa2baf449ea7caf24590488542a.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Instinctual drift</b></span> occurs when animals&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;revert&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;to&#x20;their&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;u&gt;instinctual&#x20;behaviors&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> after learning something through operant conditioning."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Instinctual drift</b></span> occurs when animals&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>revert</i>&nbsp;to their&nbsp;<u>instinctual behaviors</u></span> after learning something through operant conditioning.

"In operant conditioning, a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>partial reinforcement</b></span> <b>schedule </b>is when behavior is only reinforced <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;every&#x20;now&#x20;and&#x20;then&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Most of our own ""reinforced"" behavior is on a partially reinforced schedule.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-90637f59e77baa188ad0462f287b5243e0e49d6c.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In operant conditioning, a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;continuous&#x20;reinforcement&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>schedule </b>is when a behavior is <i>reinforced&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>every single time</u></span> it's performed."	"In operant conditioning, a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>continuous reinforcement</b></span> <b>schedule </b>is when a behavior is <i>reinforced&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>every single time</u></span> it's performed.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Acquisition is best accomplished with a continuous reinforcement schedule, since it unambigiously tells the subject which response is correct.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-90637f59e77baa188ad0462f287b5243e0e49d6c.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In operant conditioning, a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>continuous reinforcement</b></span> <b>schedule </b>is when a behavior is <i>reinforced&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;every&#x20;single&#x20;time&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> it's performed."	"In operant conditioning, a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>continuous reinforcement</b></span> <b>schedule </b>is when a behavior is <i>reinforced&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>every single time</u></span> it's performed.

"<div>A <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;variable&#x20;ratio&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> schedule is when the <u>number of responses</u> it takes to get a reward is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">an <i>inconsistent</i> number</span> that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>changes</i></span> between trials.</div>"	"<div>A <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>variable ratio</b></span> schedule is when the <u>number of responses</u> it takes to get a reward is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">an <i>inconsistent</i> number</span> that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>changes</i></span> between trials.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Using previously acquired information to draw links between what you learned previously with what you're currently learning. Think of attempting physics questions from brand new material.</div><div><br></div><img src=""BC6YKDCpJb94FPVujRZVXYy1CYc3KyGAQ3rp6NPZ_hqwe2-u-WOdzfJYy9ek_Oz5d-sAwzNk7-xREf0rI2ryE5gyZg.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Latent&#x20;learning&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when we <i>passively </i>learn how to do things from watching others, but only <i>express</i> that information when we have to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">do it ourselves</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Latent learning</b></span> is when we <i>passively </i>learn how to do things from watching others, but only <i>express</i> that information when we have to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">do it ourselves</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""While you might have learned how to cook a roast by watching your parents prepare dinner, this learning may not be apparent until you find yourself having to cook a meal on your own.""</div><div><br></div><img src=""62490628_2160282497403465_6623584532509491200_n-e1561389380306.jpg""><br></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Latent learning</b></span> is when we <i>passively </i>learn how to do things from watching others, but only <i>express</i> that information when we have to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""do&#x20;it&#x20;ourselves"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Latent learning</b></span> is when we <i>passively </i>learn how to do things from watching others, but only <i>express</i> that information when we have to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">do it ourselves</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""While you might have learned how to cook a roast by watching your parents prepare dinner, this learning may not be apparent until you find yourself having to cook a meal on your own.""</div><div><br></div><img src=""62490628_2160282497403465_6623584532509491200_n-e1561389380306.jpg""><br></div>"
"What's the <i>difference </i>between <b>demand characteristics</b> &amp; the <b>Hawthorne effect</b>?<div><br></div><div><b>Demand characteristics</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""behavior&#x20;is&#x20;changed&#x20;to&#x20;align&#x20;with&#x20;what&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;i&gt;researchers&#x20;are&#x20;expecting&#x20;to&#x20;see&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div><b>Hawthorne effect</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""behavior&#x20;is&#x20;changed&#x20;when&#x20;we&#x27;re&#x20;&lt;i&gt;being&#x20;watched&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&#x28;generally&#x29;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"What's the <i>difference </i>between <b>demand characteristics</b> &amp; the <b>Hawthorne effect</b>?<div><br></div><div><b>Demand characteristics</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">behavior is changed to align with what the <i>researchers are expecting to see</i>.</span></div><div><b>Hawthorne effect</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">behavior is changed when we're <i>being watched</i> (generally).</span></div>

<div>We assume the green bar is farther away.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6ae992ee6c82ba597e63b5e158a87cfd.png""><br></div></div>"
"In operant conditioning, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;aversive&#x20;control&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when behavior is primarily motivated by wanting to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>avoid</i>&nbsp;punishments</u></span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b><u>Negative reinforcement</u></b></div><div>- Cleaning your room to avoid getting yelled at by your parents.</div><div><br></div><img src=""slide_30-015791dffdd47587419ae879975fa602741fb1e4.jpg""></div>"
"In operant conditioning, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>aversive control</b></span> is when behavior is primarily motivated by wanting to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;avoid&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;punishments&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"In operant conditioning, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>aversive control</b></span> is when behavior is primarily motivated by wanting to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>avoid</i>&nbsp;punishments</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b><u>Negative reinforcement</u></b></div><div>- Cleaning your room to avoid getting yelled at by your parents.</div><div><br></div><img src=""slide_30-015791dffdd47587419ae879975fa602741fb1e4.jpg""></div>"
"In operant conditioning, <b>aversive control</b> is driven by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;negative&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""reinforcement"" data-ordinal=""1"">[reinforcement/punishment]</span>."	"In operant conditioning, <b>aversive control</b> is driven by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>negative</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">reinforcement</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Aversive control is when we're motivated to behave in order to <u>avoid</u> punishments.</div><div>- ""You don't have to do your chores today""</div><div><br></div><img src=""slide_30-015791dffdd47587419ae879975fa602741fb1e4.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Taste&#x20;aversion&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when you <i>avoid </i>a specific food for a long period of time after it makes you <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">sick</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Taste aversion</b></span> is when you <i>avoid </i>a specific food for a long period of time after it makes you <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">sick</span>.
