"Why do we have <b>phobias </b>against things like heights and insects but <i>not </i>against cars or technology?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Our&#x20;ancestors&#x20;were&#x20;more&#x20;likely&#x20;to&#x20;be&#x20;killed&#x20;by&#x20;high&#x20;heights&#x20;or&#x20;insects&#x20;than&#x20;cars&#x20;&#x28;seeing&#x20;as&#x20;they&#x20;didn&#x27;t&#x20;exist&#x29;&#x2E;&#x20;Became&#x20;evolutionarily&#x20;advantegeous&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Why do we have <b>phobias </b>against things like heights and insects but <i>not </i>against cars or technology?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Our ancestors were more likely to be killed by high heights or insects than cars (seeing as they didn't exist). Became evolutionarily advantegeous.</span></div>

"Three factors of&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>social cognitive theory</b></span> that <i>influence</i> our behavior:<div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Behavior"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Environment"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Perceptions"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"Three factors of&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>social cognitive theory</b></span> that <i>influence</i> our behavior:<div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Behavior</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Environment</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Perceptions</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- This model says that these 3 factors determine our behavior.</div><div>- Developed by Alfred Bandura (Bobo doll researcher).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-129a982406a4fd1186645ecf93aa803449c91e2c.jpg""><br></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Reciprocal&#x20;determinism&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> says that the three factors of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>social cognitive theory</u></span> (perception, behavior, &amp; environment) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>influence</i> each other</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Reciprocal determinism</b></span> says that the three factors of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>social cognitive theory</u></span> (perception, behavior, &amp; environment) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>influence</i> each other</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Social cognitive theory</b> says our behavior can be shaped both by our environment and perceptions, but this theory explains how each of these factors <u><b>influence each other</b></u>.</div><div><br></div><div>- Developed by Alfred Bandura (Bobo doll researcher).<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""2ae48b1b6fc5b0d4583cb1c65d15fce5.png""></div>"
"<b>Reciprocal determinism</b> &amp; the <b>social cognitive theory</b>&nbsp;of behavior were <i>both</i> developed by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Albert&#x20;Bandura"" data-ordinal=""1"">[person]</span>."	"<b>Reciprocal determinism</b> &amp; the <b>social cognitive theory</b>&nbsp;of behavior were <i>both</i> developed by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Albert Bandura</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Social cognitive theory</b> says our behavior can be shaped both by our environment and perceptions, but this theory explains how each of these factors <u><b>influence each other</b></u>.</div><div><br></div><div>- Developed by Alfred Bandura (Bobo doll researcher).<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""2ae48b1b6fc5b0d4583cb1c65d15fce5.png""></div>"
"What's the <i>difference </i>between the <b>social cognitive theory</b> &amp; <b>reciprocal determinsm</b>?<div><br></div><div><b>Social cognitive theory</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""we&#x20;learn&#x20;by&#x20;&lt;u&gt;observing&#x20;others&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&#x28;including&#x20;personal&#x20;biases&#x2C;&#x20;environmental&#x20;factors&#x2C;&#x20;and&#x20;our&#x20;behaviors&#x29;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div><b>Reciprocal determinism</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""3&#x20;factors&#x20;of&#x20;social&#x20;cognitive&#x20;theory&#x20;&lt;i&gt;influence&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;each&#x20;other&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"What's the <i>difference </i>between the <b>social cognitive theory</b> &amp; <b>reciprocal determinsm</b>?<div><br></div><div><b>Social cognitive theory</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">we learn by <u>observing others</u> (including personal biases, environmental factors, and our behaviors).</span></div><div><b>Reciprocal determinism</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">3 factors of social cognitive theory <i>influence</i> each other.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Developed by Alfred Bandura (Bobo doll researcher).<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""2ae48b1b6fc5b0d4583cb1c65d15fce5.png""></div>"
"An <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;internal&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[internal/external]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;locus&#x20;of&#x20;control&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when we believe we're <u><i>entirely </i>in control</u> of our own fate."	"An <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>internal</i></b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>locus of control</b></span> is when we believe we're <u><i>entirely </i>in control</u> of our own fate.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

""I thought that test was hard, but that's because I didn't study enough.""</div>"
"An <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;external&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[internal/external]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;locus&#x20;of&#x20;control&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when we believe we're <u><i>not</i> in control</u> of our own fate."	"An <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>external</i></b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>locus of control</b></span> is when we believe we're <u><i>not</i> in control</u> of our own fate.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

""That test was impossible!""</div>"
"""That test was <i>impossible</i>! He made it too hard!"" demonstrates an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;external&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[internal/external]</span> <b>locus of control</b>."	"""That test was <i>impossible</i>! He made it too hard!"" demonstrates an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>external</i></b></span> <b>locus of control</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Not taking accountability for your own fate.</div>"
"""That test was hard, but that's only because <i>I</i> didn't study enough"" demonstrates an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;internal&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[internal/external]</span> <b>locus of control</b>."	"""That test was hard, but that's only because <i>I</i> didn't study enough"" demonstrates an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>internal</i></b></span> <b>locus of control</b>.

<div>- CDC</div><div>- Situational attribution is how we attribute the actions of others to their situation as opposed to their internal character (opposite of dispositional attribution).</div><div><br></div><img src=""1200-563866-situational-vs-dispositional.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""de19db609180f2ff76fc386290a9ee1c.png""><br></div></div>"
"According to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Raymond&#x20;Cattell"" data-ordinal=""3"">[person]</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>source</b></span> <b>traits </b>are the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>mental processes</i></u></span> that develop into our personality."	"According to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">Raymond Cattell</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>source</b></span> <b>traits </b>are the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>mental processes</i></u></span> that develop into our personality.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>- Falls under&nbsp;<b style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">trait theory</b>&nbsp;of&nbsp;personality.</div></div><div><br></div>- Two types of traits:&nbsp;<b>surface&nbsp;</b>(observable) &amp;&nbsp;<b>source&nbsp;</b>(mental)<div><br></div><div><img src=""225px-Raymond_Cattell.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"What's the <i>difference&nbsp;</i>between stereotypic &amp; consummate behaviors?<div><br></div><div><b>Stereotypic</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""perform&#x20;the&#x20;same&#x20;way&#x20;everytime&#x20;you&#x20;do&#x20;it&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div><b>Consummate</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""behaviors&#x20;that&#x20;do&#x20;not&#x20;require&#x20;practice&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"What's the <i>difference&nbsp;</i>between stereotypic &amp; consummate behaviors?<div><br></div><div><b>Stereotypic</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">perform the same way everytime you do it.</span></div><div><b>Consummate</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">behaviors that do not require practice.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"What's the <i>differece </i>between stereotypic &amp; consummate behaviors?<div><br></div><div><b>Consummate</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""behaviors&#x20;that&#x20;do&#x20;not&#x20;require&#x20;practice&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b><br></b></div><div><b>Stereotypic</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""perform&#x20;the&#x20;same&#x20;way&#x20;everytime&#x20;you&#x20;do&#x20;it&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"What's the <i>differece </i>between stereotypic &amp; consummate behaviors?<div><br></div><div><b>Consummate</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">behaviors that do not require practice.</span><b><br></b></div><div><b>Stereotypic</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">perform the same way everytime you do it.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Crystal&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[fluid/crystal]</span>&nbsp;<b>intelligence&nbsp;</b><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>improves</i></span>&nbsp;with <u>age</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Crystal</b></span>&nbsp;<b>intelligence&nbsp;</b><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>improves</i></span>&nbsp;with <u>age</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Think of crystal intelligence as <b>wisdom</b> (improves as you get older).</div><div>- Fluid intelligence actually&nbsp;<u>worsens</u>&nbsp;with age.</div><div><br></div><img src=""fluid-intelligence-vs-crystallized-intelligence-2795004_color2-5b69d275c9e77c0050ba7290.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Crystal</b></span>&nbsp;<b>intelligence&nbsp;</b><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;improves&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[worsens/improves]</span>&nbsp;with <u>age</u>." <img src=""ArticleImageHandler.ashx""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ages 1-3<br></div><div>- Children develop feelings of control and productivity by being able to control bowel movements.</div><div>- <b>O.A.P.L.G.</b></div><div>- Developed by Frued</div><div><br></div><img src=""freuds-stages-of-psychosexual-development-2795962-5b61cd3dc9e77c007be4124d.png""></div>"

<div>- Important for <b><u>observational learning</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""gettyimages-1080455448-68ce10d3395e2c6d73c17666ad90b28140674071.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Reference&#x20;groups&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are groups of people we <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>compare </i>ourselves to</span>, like our family or kids at school."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Reference groups</b></span> are groups of people we <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>compare </i>ourselves to</span>, like our family or kids at school.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- We <u>don't</u> need to be apart of this group for it to be our reference group.<br></div><div>- <b>Reference groups</b> apply to groups of people, whereas a <b>role model</b> refers to one person.</div><div>- ""Why can't I be more like my sisters?!"" <div>Attempts to perceive yourself <u>overly favorably</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""1200-610405-33553724.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Stereotypes&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are <u>generalized beliefs</u> (either good or bad) about a group of people."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Stereotypes</b></span> are <u>generalized beliefs</u> (either good or bad) about a group of people.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""He wears glasses... he must be smart!""</div><div><br></div><div>- Advantage: helps us gather quick data about social groups.</div><div>- Disadvantage: inaccurate</div><div><br></div><img src=""08b303f94943d92262f90b807f5de381.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Stereotype&#x20;threat&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is a self-fulfilling prophecy where people experience <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>anxiety</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>worsened performance</u></span> because they're afraid of <i style="""">conforming </i>to stereotypes made about their group."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Stereotype threat</b></span> is a self-fulfilling prophecy where people experience <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>anxiety</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>worsened performance</u></span> because they're afraid of <i style="""">conforming </i>to stereotypes made about their group.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Stereotype threatens their performance.</div><div>- ""Women tend to perform worse on this exam than men,"" and then women actually <u>do</u> perform worse due to anxiety.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""40fba0a25b2d256f988b46addb3b08ac.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""08b303f94943d92262f90b807f5de381.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Stereotype threat</b></span> is a self-fulfilling prophecy where people experience <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;anxiety&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;worsened&#x20;performance&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> because they're afraid of <i style="""">conforming </i>to stereotypes made about their group."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Stereotype threat</b></span> is a self-fulfilling prophecy where people experience <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>anxiety</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>worsened performance</u></span> because they're afraid of <i style="""">conforming </i>to stereotypes made about their group.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Stereotype threatens their performance.</div><div>- ""Women tend to perform worse on this exam than men,"" and then women actually <u>do</u> perform worse due to anxiety.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""40fba0a25b2d256f988b46addb3b08ac.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""08b303f94943d92262f90b807f5de381.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Self&#x2D;fulfilling&#x20;prophecies&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are when our <i>attitudes </i>towards something cause us to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">change our behavior</span> in a way that <i>affirms </i>our stereotype."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Self-fulfilling prophecies</b></span> are when our <i>attitudes </i>towards something cause us to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">change our behavior</span> in a way that <i>affirms </i>our stereotype.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""She never talks to me so she must be rude... I'm going to avoid her."" Results in avoiding the person → they don't talk → continues believing she's rude for not talking.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""dd9ec8f2b1c1ec997854235c85268479.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""08b303f94943d92262f90b807f5de381.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Self-fulfilling prophecies</b></span> are when our <i>attitudes </i>towards something cause us to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""change&#x20;our&#x20;behavior"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in a way that <i>affirms </i>our stereotype."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Self-fulfilling prophecies</b></span> are when our <i>attitudes </i>towards something cause us to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">change our behavior</span> in a way that <i>affirms </i>our stereotype.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""She never talks to me so she must be rude... I'm going to avoid her."" Results in avoiding the person → they don't talk → continues believing she's rude for not talking.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""dd9ec8f2b1c1ec997854235c85268479.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""08b303f94943d92262f90b807f5de381.png""><br></div></div>"
"Children in Mead's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;game&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>&nbsp;stage begin to understand the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>generalized other</u></span> - i.e. society as a whole." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Grade school age.</div><div>- Realize people have multiple roles. On top of being a doctor, you can also be a mom, daughter, wife, etc.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""594571e9bf6f61e27050136368686f99.png""><br></div></div>"
"3 components of <b>prejudice</b>:<div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Cognition&#x20;&#x28;stereotyping&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Affect&#x20;&#x28;emotion&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Discrimination"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"3 components of <b>prejudice</b>:<div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Cognition (stereotyping)</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Affect (emotion)</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Discrimination</span></div>

<div>Prejudice → <b>C</b>.<b>A</b>.<b>D</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""08b303f94943d92262f90b807f5de381.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Prejudice&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i><u>negative belief</u></i></span>&nbsp;about a certain individual based on their social group."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Prejudice</b></span> is a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i><u>negative belief</u></i></span>&nbsp;about a certain individual based on their social group.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Prejudice</b></span> is a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;negative&#x20;belief&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>&nbsp;about a certain individual based on their social group." "<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Discrimination</b></span> is when you <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;take&#x20;action&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&lt;i&gt;based&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;on&#x20;your&#x20;prejudice"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;of an individual based on their social group."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Discrimination</b></span> is when you <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>take action</u> <i>based</i> on your prejudice</span>&nbsp;of an individual based on their social group.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Discrimination</b></span> is when you <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>take action</u> <i>based</i> on your prejudice</span>&nbsp;of an individual based on their social group.

<div><img src=""boss-looking-down-someone-1298702.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""08b303f94943d92262f90b807f5de381.png""><br></div></div>"

"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>frustration-aggression hypothesis</b></span> says that our <i>frustrations </i>get bottled up and lead to <u>aggressive impulses</u>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""119dReCMFSMl_G3kz_VVvEA.jpeg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""08b303f94943d92262f90b807f5de381.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;relative&#x20;deprivation&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;b&gt;hypothesis&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span>&nbsp;says that people tend to become more <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>prejudice</u></span> when they're <i>deprived </i>of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">something they want</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>relative deprivation&nbsp;</b><b>hypothesis</b></span>&nbsp;says that people tend to become more <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>prejudice</u></span> when they're <i>deprived </i>of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">something they want</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Frustration aggression hypothesis</b>: our&nbsp;<i>frustrations&nbsp;</i>get bottled up and lead to&nbsp;aggressive impulses... making us&nbsp;<i>prejudice</i>.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""08b303f94943d92262f90b807f5de381.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>relative deprivation&nbsp;</b><b>hypothesis</b></span>&nbsp;says that people tend to become more <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;prejudice&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> when they're <i>deprived </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""something&#x20;they&#x20;want"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>relative deprivation&nbsp;</b><b>hypothesis</b></span>&nbsp;says that people tend to become more <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>prejudice</u></span> when they're <i>deprived </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">something they want</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Frustration aggression hypothesis</b>: our&nbsp;<i>frustrations&nbsp;</i>get bottled up and lead to&nbsp;aggressive impulses... making us&nbsp;<i>prejudice</i>.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""08b303f94943d92262f90b807f5de381.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Social&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>stigma </b>is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>disapproval </i>from society</span>&nbsp;directed at a someone based on an out of the ordinary characteristic (mental health)."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Social</b></span> <b>stigma </b>is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>disapproval </i>from society</span>&nbsp;directed at a someone based on an out of the ordinary characteristic (mental health).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""bdc88c6e150d14664d97e888ecf90435.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Self&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>stigma </b>is when an individual <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>internalizes<i> </i>the stigma</u></span> <i>attributed</i> to them by <i>other</i> people - leading to avoidance, depression, isolation.<br>"	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Self</b></span> <b>stigma </b>is when an individual <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>internalizes<i> </i>the stigma</u></span> <i>attributed</i> to them by <i>other</i> people - leading to avoidance, depression, isolation.<br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Other people are the ones that attribute the stigma to you - you just happen to internalize it.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""bdc88c6e150d14664d97e888ecf90435.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;primacy&#x20;bias&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[bias]</span> is how the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>first impression</u></span> someone makes on us is usually the <i>most</i> important."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>primacy bias</b></span> is how the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>first impression</u></span> someone makes on us is usually the <i>most</i> important.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Don't confuse this with this primacy effect!</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1c99741b3e8c0d0a723d8ad4bf720e64.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>primacy bias</b></span> is how the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;first&#x20;impression&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> someone makes on us is usually the <i>most</i> important."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>primacy bias</b></span> is how the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>first impression</u></span> someone makes on us is usually the <i>most</i> important.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Don't confuse this with this primacy effect!</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1c99741b3e8c0d0a723d8ad4bf720e64.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;recency&#x20;bias&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[bias]</span> is how we tend to place <i>extra </i>emphasis on someone's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">recent behavior</span> when judging them (compared to their past)."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>recency bias</b></span> is how we tend to place <i>extra </i>emphasis on someone's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">recent behavior</span> when judging them (compared to their past).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Don't confuse this with this recency effect!</div><div>- The <b>primary bias</b> is still usually the strongest (first impression).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1c99741b3e8c0d0a723d8ad4bf720e64.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>recency bias</b></span> is how we tend to place <i>extra </i>emphasis on someone's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""recent&#x20;behavior"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> when judging them (compared to their past)."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>recency bias</b></span> is how we tend to place <i>extra </i>emphasis on someone's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">recent behavior</span> when judging them (compared to their past).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Don't confuse this with this recency effect!</div><div>- The <b>primary bias</b> is still usually the strongest (first impression).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1c99741b3e8c0d0a723d8ad4bf720e64.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;halo&#x20;effect&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when we assume just because someone made a <u>good impression</u>&nbsp;that they must <i>also </i>be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">good in<i> all</i> aspects</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>halo effect</b></span> is when we assume just because someone made a <u>good impression</u>&nbsp;that they must <i>also </i>be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">good in<i> all</i> aspects</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""She's funny... I bet she's also really smart!""</div><div>- Related to the <u style=""font-weight: bold;"">phsyical attractiveness stereotype</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""2795906-what-is-the-halo-effect-5ae866adc673350036f1657f-0c79f8423db61983e367f8d2b428ff6cc7e645cd.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>halo effect</b></span> is when we assume just because someone made a <u>good impression</u>&nbsp;that they must <i>also </i>be <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""good&#x20;in&lt;i&gt;&#x20;all&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;aspects"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>halo effect</b></span> is when we assume just because someone made a <u>good impression</u>&nbsp;that they must <i>also </i>be <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">good in<i> all</i> aspects</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""She's funny... I bet she's also really smart!""</div><div>- Related to the <u style=""font-weight: bold;"">phsyical attractiveness stereotype</u>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""2795906-what-is-the-halo-effect-5ae866adc673350036f1657f-0c79f8423db61983e367f8d2b428ff6cc7e645cd.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;physical&#x20;attractiveness&#x20;stereotype&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is how we assume <u>physically attractive</u> people <i>also</i> have other <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">positive personality traits</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>physical attractiveness stereotype</b></span> is how we assume <u>physically attractive</u> people <i>also</i> have other <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">positive personality traits</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Related to the&nbsp;<u><b>halo effect</b></u>.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""2795906-what-is-the-halo-effect-5ae866adc673350036f1657f-0c79f8423db61983e367f8d2b428ff6cc7e645cd.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>physical attractiveness stereotype</b></span> is how we assume <u>physically attractive</u> people <i>also</i> have other <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""positive&#x20;personality&#x20;traits"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>physical attractiveness stereotype</b></span> is how we assume <u>physically attractive</u> people <i>also</i> have other <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">positive personality traits</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Related to the&nbsp;<u><b>halo effect</b></u>.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""2795906-what-is-the-halo-effect-5ae866adc673350036f1657f-0c79f8423db61983e367f8d2b428ff6cc7e645cd.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;devil&#x20;effect&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when we assume just because someone made a&nbsp;<u>bad impression</u>&nbsp;that they must&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>also&nbsp;</i>be bad in<i>&nbsp;all</i>&nbsp;aspects</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>devil effect</b></span> is when we assume just because someone made a&nbsp;<u>bad impression</u>&nbsp;that they must&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>also&nbsp;</i>be bad in<i>&nbsp;all</i>&nbsp;aspects</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Opposite of the <b><u>halo effect</u></b>.</div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>devil effect</b></span> is when we assume just because someone made a&nbsp;<u>bad impression</u>&nbsp;that they must&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;also&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;be&#x20;bad&#x20;in&lt;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;all&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;aspects"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>devil effect</b></span> is when we assume just because someone made a&nbsp;<u>bad impression</u>&nbsp;that they must&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>also&nbsp;</i>be bad in<i>&nbsp;all</i>&nbsp;aspects</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Opposite of the <b><u>halo effect</u></b>.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Ethnography&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the <i>study </i>of a particular&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">group of people</span> using <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>qualitative</u></span>&nbsp;information, like interviews and observations." "<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Ethnocentrism&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is believing one's culture is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>superior </i>to another's</u></span>&nbsp;by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">comparing <i>their</i> cultural standards</span> through the lens of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>your own</i> cultural standards</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ethnocentrism</b></span> is believing one's culture is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>superior </i>to another's</u></span>&nbsp;by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">comparing <i>their</i> cultural standards</span> through the lens of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>your own</i> cultural standards</span>.</div>

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cultural relativism</b></span> is judging someone else's culture with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">an&nbsp;<i>unbiased&nbsp;</i>perspective</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Other group members often viewed <b><u>favorably</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""94e8053793959da2c0f0573a886fed2c.png""><br></div></div>"

"An <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>out-group</b></span> is a group of people we&nbsp;<i style="""">do not</i>&nbsp;<u>identify</u><i> </i>ourselves with, like a club we're <i>not </i>apart of.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Often viewed <b style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">unfavorably</b>, but not always.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""94e8053793959da2c0f0573a886fed2c.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;In&#x2D;group&#x20;favoritism&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when we act <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>friendly</i></span> towards people in our <u>in-group</u> but act <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>neutral</i></span> towards our <u>out-group</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>In-group favoritism</b></span> is when we act <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>friendly</i></span> towards people in our <u>in-group</u> but act <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>neutral</i></span> towards our <u>out-group</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>In contrast with <b>out-group derogation</b>, where we act extremely friendly to our in-group but discriminate against our out-group.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""94e8053793959da2c0f0573a886fed2c.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>In-group favoritism</b></span> is when we act <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;friendly&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> towards people in our <u>in-group</u> but act <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;neutral&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> towards our <u>out-group</u>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>In-group favoritism</b></span> is when we act <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>friendly</i></span> towards people in our <u>in-group</u> but act <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>neutral</i></span> towards our <u>out-group</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>In contrast with <b>out-group derogation</b>, where we act extremely friendly to our in-group but discriminate against our out-group.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""94e8053793959da2c0f0573a886fed2c.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Out&#x2D;group&#x20;derogation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when we act <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>extremely </i>friendly</span> towards people in our <u>in-group</u> but <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>discriminate</i> against</span>&nbsp;our <u>out-group</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Out-group derogation</b></span> is when we act <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>extremely </i>friendly</span> towards people in our <u>in-group</u> but <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>discriminate</i> against</span>&nbsp;our <u>out-group</u>.

"<div>3 stages of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;general&#x20;adaptation&#x20;syndrome&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>:<br></div><div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Alarm</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Resistance</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Exhaustion</span></div>"	"<div>3 stages of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>general adaptation syndrome</b></span>:<br></div><div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Alarm</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Resistance</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Exhaustion</span></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This is how we respond to a stressor.</div><div><br></div><img src=""1296x728_BODY_General_Adaptation_Syndrome.jpg""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;mere&#x20;exposure&#x20;effect&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> says that the <i>more</i>&nbsp;we see something, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> we'll like it."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>mere exposure effect</b></span> says that the <i>more</i>&nbsp;we see something, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> we'll like it.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Music, people, art, etc.<div>- ""Burn out"" can occur where the more you see something the more you hate it, but it's not that common.</div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>mere exposure effect</b></span> says that the <i>more</i>&nbsp;we see something, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[less/more]</span> we'll like it."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>mere exposure effect</b></span> says that the <i>more</i>&nbsp;we see something, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> we'll like it.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Music, people, art, etc.<div>- ""Burn out"" can occur where the more you see something the more you hate it, but it's not that common.</div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Retrograde&#x20;amnesia&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when you're&nbsp;<i style="""">able</i>&nbsp;to&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>form new memories</u></span> &amp; but you <i>can't </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">remember old memories</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Retrograde amnesia</b></span> is when you're&nbsp;<i style="""">able</i>&nbsp;to&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>form new memories</u></span> &amp; but you <i>can't </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">remember old memories</span>.</div>

"Advertisers rely on the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>mere exposure effect</b></span> to sell us products, since the <i>more </i>we see their product the <i>more </i>we'll like it.

<img src=""paste-c4fa084061f9700fa282174c9b16e99cbd6531e7.jpg""></div>"
"People tend to find <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;average&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[average/unique]</span> facial features <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> attractive than <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""unique"" data-ordinal=""1"">[average/unique]</span> facial features."	"People tend to find <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>average</u></span> facial features <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> attractive than <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">unique</span> facial features.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Suggests there's a prototype of what people are attracted to that the most average face tends to fit.</div>"
"People tend to find <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>average</u></span> facial features <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[more/less]</span> attractive than <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">unique</span> facial features."	"People tend to find <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>average</u></span> facial features <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> attractive than <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">unique</span> facial features.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Suggests there's a prototype of what people are attracted to that the most average face tends to fit.</div>"
"We're more likely to rate women as being <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[less/more]</span> <b>attractive</b> shortly after we <u>exercise</u>."	"We're more likely to rate women as being <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>more</i></span> <b>attractive</b> shortly after we <u>exercise</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"We're <i>more</i> likely to be attracted to someone with facial features <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;similar&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;to"" data-ordinal=""1"">[similar/different]</span> our own."	"We're <i>more</i> likely to be attracted to someone with facial features <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>similar </i>to</span> our own.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Similarity&#x20;bias&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[bias]</span>&nbsp;is how we tend to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>favor</i> people <u>similar</u> to us</span>&nbsp;moreso than people different from us."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Similarity bias</b></span>&nbsp;is how we tend to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>favor</i> people <u>similar</u> to us</span>&nbsp;moreso than people different from us.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Projection&#x20;bias&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[bias]</span> is when we <i>assume&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">someone shares the <u>same beliefs</u></span> as we do - even if they really don't."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Projection bias</b></span> is when we <i>assume&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">someone shares the <u>same beliefs</u></span> as we do - even if they really don't.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Projection bias</b></span> is when we <i>assume&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""someone&#x20;shares&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;u&gt;same&#x20;beliefs&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> as we do - even if they really don't."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Projection bias</b></span> is when we <i>assume&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">someone shares the <u>same beliefs</u></span> as we do - even if they really don't.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;False&#x20;consensus&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when we <i>assume </i>everyone <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>agrees with us</u></span>, even if they really don't."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>False consensus</b></span> is when we <i>assume </i>everyone <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>agrees with us</u></span>, even if they really don't.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Don't confuse this with the illusion of unanimity in groupthink (same thing but only that pertains to groupthink).</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>False consensus</b></span> is when we <i>assume </i>everyone <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;agrees&#x20;with&#x20;us&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>, even if they really don't." "What was the <b>Harlow monkey experiment</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Baby&#x20;monkeys&#x20;were&#x20;separated&#x20;from&#x20;their&#x20;mothers&#x20;and&#x20;offered&#x20;a&#x20;robot&#x20;mother&#x20;either&#x20;holding&#x20;food&#x20;or&#x20;a&#x20;blanket&#x2E;&#x20;Babies&#x20;chose&#x20;the&#x20;mother&#x20;with&#x20;a&#x20;blanket&#x20;&#x28;showing&#x20;they&#x20;value&#x20;comfort&#x20;more&#x20;than&#x20;food&#x29;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"What was the <b>Harlow monkey experiment</b>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Baby monkeys were separated from their mothers and offered a robot mother either holding food or a blanket. Babies chose the mother with a blanket (showing they value comfort more than food).</span></div>

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Institutional&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>discrimination </b>is discrimination on the behalf of an <u>organization</u>&nbsp;that <i>specifically </i>singles out one group of people." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Separate drinking fountains.</div><div><br></div><img src=""b903752ffdb678741c208a4900147d9c.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Side&#x2D;effect&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>discrimination </b>is <u><i>unintended </i>discrimination</u> that arises from seemingly neutral policies but ends up having a prejudicial effect anyway."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Side-effect</b></span> <b>discrimination </b>is <u><i>unintended </i>discrimination</u> that arises from seemingly neutral policies but ends up having a prejudicial effect anyway.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ex: asking job applicants if they have a car (since public transportation might become a problem), but it ends up discriminating against poor people that might not own cars.</div>- Type of unintentional discrimination.<div><br></div><div><img src=""960x0.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Past&#x2D;in&#x2D;present&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>discrimination </b>is discrimination from old social practices&nbsp;no longer in effect that <u><i>still </i>resonate</u> in the present."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Past-in-present</b></span> <b>discrimination </b>is discrimination from old social practices&nbsp;no longer in effect that <u><i>still </i>resonate</u> in the present.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Pro</b>: increased efficiency</div><div><b>Con</b>: workers lose touch of the overall picture</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-Characteristics.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-by-Max-Weber.jpg""></div>"
"In Max Weber's idea of an <b>ideal bureaucracy</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;hierarchies&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[component]</span> are where each person is <i>supervised </i>by someone above them."	"In Max Weber's idea of an <b>ideal bureaucracy</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>hierarchies</u></span> are where each person is <i>supervised </i>by someone above them.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Pro</b>: shows who's in command</div><div><b>Con</b>: takes away people's voices in decision making processes.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-Characteristics.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-by-Max-Weber.jpg""></div>"
"In Max Weber's idea of an <b>ideal bureaucracy</b>, having <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;written&#x20;rules&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[component]</span> clearly establishes the <i>policies </i>of the organization."	"In Max Weber's idea of an <b>ideal bureaucracy</b>, having <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>written rules</u></span> clearly establishes the <i>policies </i>of the organization.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Pro</b>: makes sure all employees are treated equally.</div><div><b>Con</b>: leads to <b>goal displacement</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-Characteristics.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-by-Max-Weber.jpg""></div>"
"In bureaucracies, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;goal&#x20;displacement&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when organizations start caring <i>more </i>about <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">following rules</span> &amp; <i>less </i>about <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">achieving their goals</span>."	"In bureaucracies, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>goal displacement</b></span> is when organizations start caring <i>more </i>about <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">following rules</span> &amp; <i>less </i>about <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">achieving their goals</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-Characteristics.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-by-Max-Weber.jpg""></div>"
"In bureaucracies, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>goal displacement</b></span> is when organizations start caring <i>more </i>about <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""following&#x20;rules"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; <i>less </i>about <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""achieving&#x20;their&#x20;goals"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"In bureaucracies, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>goal displacement</b></span> is when organizations start caring <i>more </i>about <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">following rules</span> &amp; <i>less </i>about <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">achieving their goals</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-Characteristics.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-by-Max-Weber.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Max&#x20;Weber&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[person]</span> was the sociologist that studied the factors that make for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ideal bureaucracies</u></span>, while also contributing to conflict theory."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Max Weber</b></span> was the sociologist that studied the factors that make for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ideal bureaucracies</u></span>, while also contributing to conflict theory.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Also contributed to <b><u>conflict theory</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-Characteristics.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-by-Max-Weber.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Max Weber</b></span> was the sociologist that studied the factors that make for <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;ideal&#x20;bureaucracies&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>, while also contributing to conflict theory."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Max Weber</b></span> was the sociologist that studied the factors that make for <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ideal bureaucracies</u></span>, while also contributing to conflict theory.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Also contributed to <b><u>conflict theory</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-Characteristics.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-by-Max-Weber.jpg""></div>"
"In Max Weber's idea of an&nbsp;<b>ideal bureaucracy</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;impersonality&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[component]</span> allows procedures be&nbsp;<i>standardized </i>&amp; unbiased."	"In Max Weber's idea of an&nbsp;<b>ideal bureaucracy</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>impersonality</u></span> allows procedures be&nbsp;<i>standardized </i>&amp; unbiased.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Pros</b>: allows for the equal treatment of all.</div><div><b>Cons</b>: can alienate employees &amp; discourage loyalty to the group.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-Characteristics.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-by-Max-Weber.jpg""></div>"
"In bureaucracies, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Peter&#x20;Principle&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> says that employees are <i>only </i>at the position they're at because they <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>aren't good enough</i> to get <u>promoted</span></u>."	"In bureaucracies, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Peter Principle</b></span> says that employees are <i>only </i>at the position they're at because they <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>aren't good enough</i> to get <u>promoted</span></u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Happens as a result of only hiring based on <b>qualifications</b> (as opposed to favoritism).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-Characteristics.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-by-Max-Weber.jpg""></div>"
"In bureaucracies, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Peter Principle</b></span> says that employees are <i>only </i>at the position they're at because they <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;aren&#x27;t&#x20;good&#x20;enough&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;to&#x20;get&#x20;&lt;u&gt;promoted"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></u>."	"In bureaucracies, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Peter Principle</b></span> says that employees are <i>only </i>at the position they're at because they <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>aren't good enough</i> to get <u>promoted</span></u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Happens as a result of only hiring based on <b>qualifications</b> (as opposed to favoritism).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-Characteristics.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""Bureaucratic-Theory-by-Max-Weber.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Foraging"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when animals search for <u>food</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Foraging</span> is when animals search for <u>food</u>.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Random&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b> mating</b> is where individuals in a population all mate <i>randomly </i>with each other - ensuring lots of <u>genetic diversity</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Random</b></span><b> mating</b> is where individuals in a population all mate <i>randomly </i>with each other - ensuring lots of <u>genetic diversity</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This is assumed in the <b><u>Hardy-Weinberg equilirbium</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""random-mating.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Assortative&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b> mating</b> is non-random mating when individuals in a population mate with others with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>similar</i></span> <u>phenotypes</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Assortative</b></span><b> mating</b> is non-random mating when individuals in a population mate with others with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>similar</i></span> <u>phenotypes</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""PuDyE_bWQLduwSngkNRS1c-iuOWajIWXi_HBOTACzs7SemsSRwP9hUVgzpzOPHUMhuSDGoOGR7VZo_106pzjn98VwYA0EHWVuhJ6H_wf5EzpvlZRUB8.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Assortative</b></span><b> mating</b> is non-random mating when individuals in a population mate with others with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;similar&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[similar/dissimilar]</span> <u>phenotypes</u>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Assortative</b></span><b> mating</b> is non-random mating when individuals in a population mate with others with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>similar</i></span> <u>phenotypes</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""PuDyE_bWQLduwSngkNRS1c-iuOWajIWXi_HBOTACzs7SemsSRwP9hUVgzpzOPHUMhuSDGoOGR7VZo_106pzjn98VwYA0EHWVuhJ6H_wf5EzpvlZRUB8.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Disassortative&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b> mating</b> is non-random mating when individuals in a population mate with others with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>dissimilar</i></span> <u>phenotypes</u>." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Attracted to individuals with <u>different</u> phenotypes.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""7yX4a0Sz6vxTHhUsOtYEEOHXBpIlZti96U_72PAl5U5geYBtiFgECkD_CLEut0GLzWsVNfXl0M1559Bp_0VUfcdCrjm6nkXWW_qeUQKK3coth_BHAFR.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Disassortative</b></span><b> mating</b> is non-random mating when individuals in a population mate with others with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;dissimilar&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[similar/dissimilar]</span> <u>phenotypes</u>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Disassortative</b></span><b> mating</b> is non-random mating when individuals in a population mate with others with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>dissimilar</i></span> <u>phenotypes</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Attracted to individuals with <u>different</u> phenotypes.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""7yX4a0Sz6vxTHhUsOtYEEOHXBpIlZti96U_72PAl5U5geYBtiFgECkD_CLEut0GLzWsVNfXl0M1559Bp_0VUfcdCrjm6nkXWW_qeUQKK3coth_BHAFR.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Scienctists believed <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;assortative&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[assortative/disassortative]</span> <b>mating </b>is the best, since it <i>increases </i><u>inclusive fitness</u>."	"Scienctists believed <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>assortative</b></span> <b>mating </b>is the best, since it <i>increases </i><u>inclusive fitness</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Inclusive fitness</b>: ability of an organism to pass on their genes (including the genes of the organism's close relatives).</div><div><br></div><div><div><img src=""00e2aa0dd2464476f301c2ba2db9c5d9.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""PuDyE_bWQLduwSngkNRS1c-iuOWajIWXi_HBOTACzs7SemsSRwP9hUVgzpzOPHUMhuSDGoOGR7VZo_106pzjn98VwYA0EHWVuhJ6H_wf5EzpvlZRUB8.jpg""></div></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Inclusive&#x20;fitness&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the ability of an organism to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>pass on</i> their <u>genes</u></span> - including the genes of the organism's close relatives.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Inclusive fitness</b></span> is the ability of an organism to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>pass on</i> their <u>genes</u></span> - including the genes of the organism's close relatives.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Includes <b>direct </b>&amp; <b>indirect fitness</b> (hence ""inclusive"").</div><div>- This is higher with <b>assortative mating</b> (since it includes the genes of the organism's close relatives).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""00e2aa0dd2464476f301c2ba2db9c5d9.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""PuDyE_bWQLduwSngkNRS1c-iuOWajIWXi_HBOTACzs7SemsSRwP9hUVgzpzOPHUMhuSDGoOGR7VZo_106pzjn98VwYA0EHWVuhJ6H_wf5EzpvlZRUB8.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Inclusive fitness</b></span> is the ability of an organism to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;pass&#x20;on&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;their&#x20;&lt;u&gt;genes&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> - including the genes of the organism's close relatives.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Inclusive fitness</b></span> is the ability of an organism to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>pass on</i> their <u>genes</u></span> - including the genes of the organism's close relatives.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Includes <b>direct </b>&amp; <b>indirect fitness</b> (hence ""inclusive"").</div><div>- This is higher with <b>assortative mating</b> (since it includes the genes of the organism's close relatives).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""00e2aa0dd2464476f301c2ba2db9c5d9.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""PuDyE_bWQLduwSngkNRS1c-iuOWajIWXi_HBOTACzs7SemsSRwP9hUVgzpzOPHUMhuSDGoOGR7VZo_106pzjn98VwYA0EHWVuhJ6H_wf5EzpvlZRUB8.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Inclusive&#x20;fitness&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> includes <i>both</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>direct fitness</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>indirect fitness</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Inclusive fitness</b></span> includes <i>both</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>direct fitness</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>indirect fitness</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""00e2aa0dd2464476f301c2ba2db9c5d9.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Inclusive fitness</b></span> includes <i>both</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;direct&#x20;fitness&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;indirect&#x20;fitness&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Inclusive fitness</b></span> includes <i>both</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>direct fitness</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>indirect fitness</u></span>.

"In <b>inclusive fitness</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""direct&#x20;fitness"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the number of offspring <i><u>you yourself</u></i> produce." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""00e2aa0dd2464476f301c2ba2db9c5d9.png""></div>"

"In <b>inclusive fitness</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">indirect fitness</span> is the number of <i>close relatives</i> you help with <u>altruistic behavior</u> - helping them pass on their genes.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""00e2aa0dd2464476f301c2ba2db9c5d9.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Evolutionary&#x20;game&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span> attempts to <i>predict</i> which traits will become <u>more common</u> in <i>successive</i> generations - based on whether or not the genes are beneficial.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Evolutionary game theory</b></span> attempts to <i>predict</i> which traits will become <u>more common</u> in <i>successive</i> generations - based on whether or not the genes are beneficial.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Helps us predict which traits we should see more of in following generations.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""400px-Game_Diagram_AniFin.gif""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""00e2aa0dd2464476f301c2ba2db9c5d9.png""></div>"

"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>macro</b></span><b>scopic </b>scale of sociology deals with <u>large institutions</u> &amp; <u>populations</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b>Functionalism </b>&amp; <b>conflict theory</b>.</div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;micro&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><b>scopic </b>scale of sociology deals with <u>face-to-face</u> interactions."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>micro</b></span><b>scopic </b>scale of sociology deals with <u>face-to-face</u> interactions.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- <b><u>Symbolic interactionism</u></b></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Functionalism&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[social theory]</span> is a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">macro</span>scopic sociological theory that looks at how <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>institutions <i>adapt</i></u></span> to maintain <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">dyanamic equilibrium</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Functionalism</b></span> is a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">macro</span>scopic sociological theory that looks at how <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>institutions <i>adapt</i></u></span> to maintain <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">dyanamic equilibrium</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

""She's just kooky"" turns into ""she's a schizophrenic""</div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;sick&#x20;role&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in society is a form of deviance, where individuals are expected to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>want</i> to get better</u></span> while also being obligated to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>return to work</u></span> once they're healthy.</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>sick role</b></span> in society is a form of deviance, where individuals are expected to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>want</i> to get better</u></span> while also being obligated to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>return to work</u></span> once they're healthy.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Developed by Talcott Parsons.</div><div>- While they're sick, they're allowed to be exempt from their normal duties.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-37ee49d2d9402a124fd39318e6d115349f8ae3f7.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""sick-person.jpg""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;illness&#x20;experience&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is how people <i>adjust </i>to <u>being sick</u>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>illness experience</b></span> is how people <i>adjust </i>to <u>being sick</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Prolonged illnesses can shape a person's self-identity.<br></div><div>- Falls under the <b>symbolic interactionist</b> perspective (i.e. how you assign meaning to things).</div></div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: <u>Police department</u>...</div><div><b>Manifest </b>function: protects us.</div><div><b>Latent </b>function: police brutality.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6cf2cb8319ade53b5c8574c35d9e92b9.png""></div>"
"<div>In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>functionalism</u></span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;manifest&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;functions&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[funct./dysfun.]</span> are the <i>intended</i> &amp; <i>positive&nbsp;</i>functions of an institution.</div><div><b></b></div>"	"<div>In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>functionalism</u></span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>manifest</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>functions</b></span> are the <i>intended</i> &amp; <i>positive&nbsp;</i>functions of an institution.</div><div><b></b></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Ex: <u>Police department</u>...</div><div><b>Manifest </b>function: protects us.</div><div><b>Latent </b>function: police brutality.</div><div><br></div><img src=""6cf2cb8319ade53b5c8574c35d9e92b9.png""></div>"
"<div>In <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;functionalism&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[social theory]</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>latent</b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>dysfunctions</b></span> are the <i>unintended </i>&amp;<i> negative&nbsp;</i>consequences of an institution, like police brutality.</div>"	"<div>In <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>functionalism</u></span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>latent</b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>dysfunctions</b></span> are the <i>unintended </i>&amp;<i> negative&nbsp;</i>consequences of an institution, like police brutality.</div>

<div>- <b><u>Functionalism</u></b></div><div>- In contrast, he felt <b>large </b>societies are held together through forced mutual independence (becoming specialized &amp; independent).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6cf2cb8319ade53b5c8574c35d9e92b9.png""><br></div></div>"

"Emile Durkheim believed <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">large</span> societies are held together through people becoming <u>specialized</u> &amp; <u>independent</u> in their work.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Social stratification refers to how we ""categorize"" people in society.</div><div>- Max Weber was also the one that outlined the ideal bureaucracy.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-3c09dd51671bc7efa15a73aee340128ec3cd19bb.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6cf2cb8319ade53b5c8574c35d9e92b9.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>The 3 factors of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>social stratification</b></span> according to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Max Weber</span>, which dictate our <i>reactions </i>to <u>social inequality</u>:</div><div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Class"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Status"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Power"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>The 3 factors of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>social stratification</b></span> according to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Max Weber</span>, which dictate our <i>reactions </i>to <u>social inequality</u>:</div><div><br></div><div>1. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Class</span></div><div>2. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Status</span></div><div>3. <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Power</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Social stratification refers to how we ""categorize"" people in society.</div><div>- Max Weber was also the one that outlined the ideal bureaucracy.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-3c09dd51671bc7efa15a73aee340128ec3cd19bb.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6cf2cb8319ade53b5c8574c35d9e92b9.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Weak&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[weak/strong]</span> <b>social constructionism</b> says <u>brute facts</u> &amp; <u>institutional facts</u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>are</i></span> real &amp; are <i>not</i> social constructs."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Weak</b></span> <b>social constructionism</b> says <u>brute facts</u> &amp; <u>institutional facts</u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>are</i></span> real &amp; are <i>not</i> social constructs.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Brute facts</b>: most basic phenomenon that governs existence (atomic theory) that <u>absolutely</u> do exist.</div><div><b>Institutional facts</b>: facts that rely on other facts (we socially assign value to money, but it still depends on the paper it's printed on).</div><div><br></div><img src=""6cf2cb8319ade53b5c8574c35d9e92b9.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Weak</b></span> <b>social constructionism</b> says <u>brute facts</u> &amp; <u>institutional facts</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;are&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[are/are not]</span> real &amp; are <i>not</i> social constructs."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Weak</b></span> <b>social constructionism</b> says <u>brute facts</u> &amp; <u>institutional facts</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>are</i></span> real &amp; are <i>not</i> social constructs.

<div><img src=""maxresdefault-f71fa1416dc4d05d9f7b4c8449483d3f82e8a261.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Fecundity&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[fertility/fecundity]</span> refers to a <i>single </i>female's <u>reproductive potential</u> for having offspring."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Fecundity</b></span> refers to a <i>single </i>female's <u>reproductive potential</u> for having offspring.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>Fertility</b>: ability for humans to actually give birth (per 1,000 individuals).<div><b>Fecundity</b>: reproductive&nbsp;<u>potential</u>&nbsp;for having offspring.</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Fertility&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[fertility/fecundity]</span> refers to the <u>actual </u><i><u>production</u> </i>of offspring from a population of 1,000 females."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Fertility</b></span> refers to the <u>actual </u><i><u>production</u> </i>of offspring from a population of 1,000 females.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Crude birth rate (CBR)</b>: number of live births per year per 1,000 individuals.</div><div>- <b>Total fertility rate (TFR)</b>: average number of children born to a women in her lifetime.</div><b><div><b><br></b></div>Fertility</b>: ability for humans to actually give birth (per 1,000 individuals).<div><b>Fecundity</b>: reproductive <u>potential</u> to have offspring of one single female.</div><div><br></div><div>- This <i><u>is</u></i> factored into population dynamics (fecundity is not).</div></div>"
"Birth, death, and migration <i>rates </i>are measured per <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1&#x2C;000"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> people over <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1&#x20;year"" data-ordinal=""1"">[time frame]</span>."	"Birth, death, and migration <i>rates </i>are measured per <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1,000</span> people over <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1 year</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Accounts for the size of the population as well (since it's per 1,000 people).</div><div>- 46 births/1k people is significantly higher than 20 births/1k people</div><div><br></div><img src=""4ab494c32eddf90197d718d6858d966a.png""></div>"
"To find the total population <i>increase</i> rate, <u>add</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""births"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""immigration"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> together and <u>divide</u> by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1&#x2C;000"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"To find the total population <i>increase</i> rate, <u>add</u> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">births</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">immigration</span> together and <u>divide</u> by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1,000</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Divide by 1,000 since it's per 1,000 people.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""4ab494c32eddf90197d718d6858d966a.png""><br></div></div>"
