"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>optic chiasm</b></span> is the place at which <i>signals </i>from the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;optic&#x20;nerves&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[structures]</span> from our left &amp; right eyes <i>cross-over</i> to reach different halves of the brain.<br></div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>optic chiasm</b></span> is the place at which <i>signals </i>from the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>optic nerves</u></span> from our left &amp; right eyes <i>cross-over</i> to reach different halves of the brain.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Optic nerve</b>: carries both signals (green &amp; purple) before reaching the optic chiasm.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Optic tract</b>: carries the ""organized"" signals (right with right, left with left) past the optic chiasm to reach the brain.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""800875_376799_ans_9c7e4b4ae89d4c7ea75005226f5339e1.jpg""></div></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>bipolar cells</b></span> that transmit information from our rods &amp; cones are <i>supported </i>by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;horizontal&#x20;cells&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[cells]</span>, which help detect <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;visual&#x20;contrasts&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>bipolar cells</b></span> that transmit information from our rods &amp; cones are <i>supported </i>by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>horizontal cells</b></span>, which help detect <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>visual contrasts</u></span>.<br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Optic nerve</b>: carries both signals (green &amp; purple) before reaching the optic chiasm.</div><div>- <b>Optic tract</b>: carries the ""organized"" signals (right with right, left with left) past the optic chiasm to reach the brain.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""800875_376799_ans_9c7e4b4ae89d4c7ea75005226f5339e1.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;world&#x2D;systems&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span> is the theory that categorizes each country as being a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">core</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">periphery</span>, or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">semi-periphery</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>world-systems theory</b></span> is the theory that categorizes each country as being a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">core</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">periphery</span>, or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">semi-periphery</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Suggests there are significant differences in social &amp; cultural issues across countries based on their economic statuses.<b><br></b></div><b><div><b><br></b></div>Core</b>: western europe &amp; USA<div><b>Periphery</b>: Latin America &amp; Africa</div><div><b>Semi-periphery</b>: India &amp; Brazil</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-617d4572b43baf942748d4d9a6f8013c903bee97.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>world-systems theory</b></span> is the theory that categorizes each country as being a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""core"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""periphery"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>, or <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""semi&#x2D;periphery"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>world-systems theory</b></span> is the theory that categorizes each country as being a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">core</span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">periphery</span>, or <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">semi-periphery</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Suggests there are significant differences in social &amp; cultural issues across countries based on their economic statuses.<b><br></b></div><b><div><b><br></b></div>Core</b>: western europe &amp; USA<div><b>Periphery</b>: Latin America &amp; Africa</div><div><b>Semi-periphery</b>: India &amp; Brazil</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-617d4572b43baf942748d4d9a6f8013c903bee97.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"In <u>globalism</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>modernization theory</b></span> says <i>every </i>country follows a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">similar path</span> to developing a <u>modern society</u>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"In <u>globalism</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>modernization theory</b></span> says <i>every </i>country follows a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""similar&#x20;path"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to developing a <u>modern society</u>."	"In <u>globalism</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>modernization theory</b></span> says <i>every </i>country follows a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">similar path</span> to developing a <u>modern society</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;dependency&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span> says <i>periphery </i>countries survive by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>exporting <i>resources</i></u></span> to <i>core </i>countries but lack the ability to develop."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dependency theory</b></span> says <i>periphery </i>countries survive by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>exporting <i>resources</i></u></span> to <i>core </i>countries but lack the ability to develop.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Related to the <b>world-systems theory</b> (core, periphery, semi-periphery).</div>- <b>Periphery</b> countries: Latin America &amp; Africa<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-617d4572b43baf942748d4d9a6f8013c903bee97.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dependency theory</b></span> says <i>periphery </i>countries survive by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>exporting <i>resources</i></u></span> to <i>core </i>countries but lack the ability to develop.

"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;skeptical&#x20;perspective&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> believes only <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>major </i>countries</u></span>&nbsp;have been fully integrated into the world economy from globalism... 3<sup>rd</sup> world countries <u>left out</u>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>skeptical perspective</b></span> believes only <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>major </i>countries</u></span>&nbsp;have been fully integrated into the world economy from globalism... 3<sup>rd</sup> world countries <u>left out</u>.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Social exclusion</b></span> is when a particular group of people are <i>excluded </i>from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">getting the <u>same opportunities</u></span> as people from <i>different </i>groups.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Intersectionality&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> describes how in order to get an accurate picture of our <u>social identities</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>all</i> of our different social roles</span>&nbsp;need to be looked at <i>together</i>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Intersectionality</b></span> describes how in order to get an accurate picture of our <u>social identities</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>all</i> of our different social roles</span>&nbsp;need to be looked at <i>together</i>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""new-piktochart_172_1ba3c3f61d9f950cc735f9cab2fba006081686dc-1-420x330.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Intersectionality</b></span> describes how in order to get an accurate picture of our <u>social identities</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;all&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;of&#x20;our&#x20;different&#x20;social&#x20;roles"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>&nbsp;need to be looked at <i>together</i>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Intersectionality</b></span> describes how in order to get an accurate picture of our <u>social identities</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>all</i> of our different social roles</span>&nbsp;need to be looked at <i>together</i>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""new-piktochart_172_1ba3c3f61d9f950cc735f9cab2fba006081686dc-1-420x330.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Social&#x20;support&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when individuals receive <u>emotional support</u> from others in their social group."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Social support</b></span> is when individuals receive <u>emotional support</u> from others in their social group.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

This is <u>different</u> from <b>social capital</b>, since social capital focues on tangible benefits you receive from having social connections.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-73ca0304e54c3cbb0cb90a084193f68f890202fa.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>What's the <i>difference</i> between <b>social capital</b> &amp; <b>social support</b>?</div><div><br></div><div><b>Social support</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;Emotional&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;support&#x20;we&#x20;receive&#x20;from&#x20;close&#x20;social&#x20;groups&#x20;&#x28;friends&#x29;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div><b>Social capital</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Tangible&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;benefits&#x20;we&#x20;receive&#x20;as&#x20;a&#x20;result&#x20;of&#x20;our&#x20;social&#x20;connections&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>What's the <i>difference</i> between <b>social capital</b> &amp; <b>social support</b>?</div><div><br></div><div><b>Social support</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>Emotional</i> support we receive from close social groups (friends).</span></div><div><b>Social capital</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">Tangible</i> benefits we receive as a result of our social connections.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-3b40de4dbd37983c15c3ad8a736ecc41c81d641c.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Karl&#x20;Marx"" data-ordinal=""1"">[person]</span> studied the different types of <b>capital</b>, like social capital, economic capital, human capital, etc."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Karl Marx</span> studied the different types of <b>capital</b>, like social capital, economic capital, human capital, etc.

"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Thomas&#x20;theorem&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> says that our <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>peceptions</u></span> of things cause us to act in a way that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">creates&nbsp;<i>real</i>&nbsp;self-fulfilling consequences</span> based on those perceptions." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hypervigilance</b></span> is a state of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;heighted&#x20;alertness&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> - typically associated with anxiety disorders." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Chronic stress increases hypervigilance in the <b>pre-frontal cortex</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""7abdb7b296ff6f7cd158b4e3ecdf15aa.jpg""></div>"
"Due to chronic stress, <u>dendrite&nbsp;growth</u> is <i>altered&nbsp;</i>on neurons in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;amygdala&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[brain region]</span>, allowing for <i>easier </i>excitation - creating heightened anxiety."	"Due to chronic stress, <u>dendrite&nbsp;growth</u> is <i>altered&nbsp;</i>on neurons in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>amygdala</b></span>, allowing for <i>easier </i>excitation - creating heightened anxiety.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""7abdb7b296ff6f7cd158b4e3ecdf15aa.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Due to chronic stress, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;dendritic&#x20;branching&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>pre-frontal cortex</b></span> becomes <i>inhibited</i> - resulting in <i>worsened </i>coping mechanisms."	"Due to chronic stress, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>dendritic branching</u></span> in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>pre-frontal cortex</b></span> becomes <i>inhibited</i> - resulting in <i>worsened </i>coping mechanisms.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Also associated with hypervigilance (heightened arousal).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""7abdb7b296ff6f7cd158b4e3ecdf15aa.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Due to chronic stress, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>dendritic branching</u></span> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;pre&#x2D;frontal&#x20;cortex&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[brain region]</span> becomes <i>inhibited</i> - resulting in <i>worsened </i>coping mechanisms."	"Due to chronic stress, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>dendritic branching</u></span> in the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>pre-frontal cortex</b></span> becomes <i>inhibited</i> - resulting in <i>worsened </i>coping mechanisms.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Also associated with hypervigilance (heightened arousal).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""7abdb7b296ff6f7cd158b4e3ecdf15aa.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Due to chronic stress, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;inflammation&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <i>impeded </i><u>dendritic branching</u> in the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>hippocampus</b></span> result in emotional changes &amp; worsened learning." <div>...because they involve disruptions in <b>memory</b> &amp; <b>self-identity</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""800.jpg""><br></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;expectancy&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span> of motivation says people are motivated to act based on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>expected payoffs</u></span> of their actions."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>expectancy theory</b></span> of motivation says people are motivated to act based on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>expected payoffs</u></span> of their actions.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>People are motivated to spend a lot of time studying because they expect that their effort will pay off.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""79f28d1d9c6c0384fa18f2d23af583ea.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>expectancy theory</b></span> of motivation says people are motivated to act based on the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;expected&#x20;payoffs&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of their actions."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>expectancy theory</b></span> of motivation says people are motivated to act based on the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>expected payoffs</u></span> of their actions.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>People are motivated to spend a lot of time studying because they expect that their effort will pay off.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""79f28d1d9c6c0384fa18f2d23af583ea.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In <u>expectancy theory</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;expectancy&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> component is believing that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">your <u>effort</u> will <i>allow</i> you to perform</span>.</div>"	"<div>In <u>expectancy theory</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>expectancy</b></span> component is believing that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">your <u>effort</u> will <i>allow</i> you to perform</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Expectancy</b> → effort allows you to perform.</div><div>- <b>Instrumentality</b> → performing will make you feel good.</div><div>- <b>Valence</b> → feeling good.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""79f28d1d9c6c0384fa18f2d23af583ea.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In <u>expectancy theory</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;instrumentality&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> component is believing that your <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>performance</u> will make you <i>feel</i> rewarded</span>.</div>"	"<div>In <u>expectancy theory</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>instrumentality</b></span> component is believing that your <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>performance</u> will make you <i>feel</i> rewarded</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Expectancy</b>&nbsp;→ effort allows you to perform.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Instrumentality</b>&nbsp;→ performing will make you feel good.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Valence</b>&nbsp;→ feeling good.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""79f28d1d9c6c0384fa18f2d23af583ea.png""></div></div></div>"
"<div>In <u>expectancy theory</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;valence&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> component is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">positive emotion</span>&nbsp;one feels after accomplishing their goal.<br></div>"	"<div>In <u>expectancy theory</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>valence</b></span> component is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">positive emotion</span>&nbsp;one feels after accomplishing their goal.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Valence</b> could be either <u>positive</u> or <u>negative</u>. After accomplishing a goal, it's positive.</div><div><br></div><div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Expectancy</b>&nbsp;→ effort allows you to perform.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Instrumentality</b>&nbsp;→ performing will make you feel good.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Valence</b>&nbsp;→ feeling good.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""79f28d1d9c6c0384fa18f2d23af583ea.png""></div></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Trait&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[personality theory]</span> attempts to explain personality through <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>observable</i> personality traits</u></span> that remain <i>consistent</i> over long periods of time."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Trait theory</b></span> attempts to explain personality through <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>observable</i> personality traits</u></span> that remain <i>consistent</i> over long periods of time.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""22f91b12571b52dd019bd21d2a61775e.png""><br></div></div>"
"Gestalt's <b>law of </b><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;invariance&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> says that even if an object is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">twitsted or distorted</span>, we can <i>still </i>recognize it as the <u>same object</u>." "<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Place theory</b></span> refers to how sound waves with <i>different</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;frequencies&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> stimulate <i>different</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;hair&#x20;cells&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in the cochlea - causing us to hear different sounds.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Place theory</b></span> refers to how sound waves with <i>different</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>frequencies</u></span> stimulate <i>different</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>hair cells</u></span> in the cochlea - causing us to hear different sounds.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Also known as <b>basilar tuning</b>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""61b929a39c284b54933dae46d3f8667f.png""><br></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Neuroleptic</u> medications</span>&nbsp;perscribed to <b>schizophrenic</b> individuals aim to <i>reduce</i> their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""positive"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-/cognitive]</span> symptoms - inadvertently <i>increasing</i> their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""negative"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-]</span> symptoms."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Neuroleptic</u> medications</span>&nbsp;perscribed to <b>schizophrenic</b> individuals aim to <i>reduce</i> their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">positive</span> symptoms - inadvertently <i>increasing</i> their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">negative</span> symptoms.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>These depress the nervous system, so they can actually make negative symptoms worse as a side-effect.</div><div><br></div><img src=""what-are-the-symptoms-of-schizophrenia-2953120-cba74c5e1dd942ecafde1824217603f9.png""></div>"

<div>Considered an interactionist in language development.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-665cb5592c817a80b13058a62c2e21a99f195fe4.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Linguistic&#x20;relativism&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[linguistic theory]</span> believes that our <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>language</u>&nbsp;can <i>influence</i>&nbsp;our <u>cognition</u></span> <i>without</i> entirely controlling it."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Linguistic relativism</b></span> believes that our <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>language</u>&nbsp;can <i>influence</i>&nbsp;our <u>cognition</u></span> <i>without</i> entirely controlling it.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Based this on their belief that there are some differences between languages (which explains why language might influence cognition without entirely controlling it).<br></div><div>- In contrast with&nbsp;<b>linguistic determinism</b>, which says language entirely controls our thoughts.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-665cb5592c817a80b13058a62c2e21a99f195fe4.jpg""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Sensitive period</b>: point in erly development that can significantly influence physiology or behavior later in life.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d43ca5b82753eaefe6ce73f33ff4d5e971c2f336.jpg""></div>"
"Reinforcing a behavior after <u>every&nbsp;correct responses</u> to something is an example of a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;fixed&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[variable/fixed]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ratio&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[interval/ratio]</span> schedule."	"Reinforcing a behavior after <u>every&nbsp;correct responses</u> to something is an example of a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>fixed</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ratio</b></span> schedule.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Fixed</b> <b>ratio</b> because you're given a reward after the same number of correct responses (1 correct response → reward).</div><div>- A system where behavior was reinforced with a <u>different reward</u> each time would be a variable ratio schedule.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-90637f59e77baa188ad0462f287b5243e0e49d6c.jpg""></div>"
"Behaviors reinforced through <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;variable&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[variable/fixed]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;interval&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ratio/interval]</span> <b>schedules</b> are <u>performed <i>consistently</i></u> over time (moderately paced)."	"Behaviors reinforced through <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>variable</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>interval</b></span> <b>schedules</b> are <u>performed <i>consistently</i></u> over time (moderately paced).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-23679ba4266ed33216e821a33180a4aff87c812f.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Behaviors reinforced through <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;variable&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[variable/fixed]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ratio&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ratio/interval]</span> <b>schedules</b> are performed <i>consistently</i> &amp; <i>rapidly</i>."	"Behaviors reinforced through <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>variable</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ratio</b></span> <b>schedules</b> are performed <i>consistently</i> &amp; <i>rapidly</i>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Think of playing a slot machine. You'll be there consistently (because you're always expecting to win on the next turn), &amp; rapidly (because you want to win as quickly as possible).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-23679ba4266ed33216e821a33180a4aff87c812f.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Behaviors reinforced through <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;fixed&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[variable/fixed]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;interval&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ratio/interval]</span> <b>schedules</b> are performed the most <i>right</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>before</i></span> receiving the reward."	"Behaviors reinforced through <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>fixed</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>interval</b></span> <b>schedules</b> are performed the most <i>right</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>before</i></span> receiving the reward.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>If you were in a weight loss competition with weekly weigh-ins, you'd ""sprint"" closer towards the end of every week.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-23679ba4266ed33216e821a33180a4aff87c812f.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Behaviors reinforced through <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>fixed</b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>interval</b></span> <b>schedules</b> are performed the most <i>right</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;before&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[before/after]</span> receiving the reward."	"Behaviors reinforced through <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>fixed</b></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>interval</b></span> <b>schedules</b> are performed the most <i>right</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>before</i></span> receiving the reward.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>If you were in a weight loss competition with weekly weigh-ins, you'd ""sprint"" closer towards the end of every week.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-23679ba4266ed33216e821a33180a4aff87c812f.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Behaviors reinforced through <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;fixed&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[variable/fixed]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ratio&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ratio/interval]</span> <b>schedules</b> are performed <i>rapidly</i> &amp; with a <i>slight pause</i> after the last reward."	"Behaviors reinforced through <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>fixed</b></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ratio</b></span> <b>schedules</b> are performed <i>rapidly</i> &amp; with a <i>slight pause</i> after the last reward.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-23679ba4266ed33216e821a33180a4aff87c812f.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>door in the face</b></span> <b>technique</b> says people are <i>more</i> likely to agree to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""small"" data-ordinal=""2"">[small/large]</span> favors <i>after</i> having <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;rejected&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;a&#x20;larger&#x20;favor&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <i>first</i>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>door in the face</b></span> <b>technique</b> says people are <i>more</i> likely to agree to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">small</span> favors <i>after</i> having <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>rejected</i> a larger favor</u></span> <i>first</i>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-a7fa088198b6edcafa107a0ba0cd5e862e84096a.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-5c4808abe22427042eced7eee26de3f8ff71896a.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Reciprocal determinism</b></span> says that the three factors of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;social&#x20;cognitive&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[theory]</span> (perception, behavior, &amp; environment) <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;influence&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;each&#x20;other"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Reciprocal determinism</b></span> says that the three factors of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>social cognitive theory</u></span> (perception, behavior, &amp; environment) <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>influence</i> each other</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Social cognitive theory</b> says our behavior can be shaped both by our environment and perceptions, but this theory explains how each of these factors <u><b>influence each other</b></u>.</div><div><br></div><div>- Developed by Alfred Bandura (Bobo doll researcher).<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""2ae48b1b6fc5b0d4583cb1c65d15fce5.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;overconfidence&#x20;bias&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> happens when people become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>extremely</i> confident in their beliefs</u></span>&nbsp;depsite not truly knowing that much."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>overconfidence bias</b></span> happens when people become <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>extremely</i> confident in their beliefs</u></span>&nbsp;depsite not truly knowing that much.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Social&#x20;constructionism&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[social theory]</span> says that reality is created through <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>social interactions</u></span> &amp; <i>maintained</i> through <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>agreed-upon meanings</u></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Social constructionism</b></span> says that reality is created through <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>social interactions</u></span> &amp; <i>maintained</i> through <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>agreed-upon meanings</u></span>.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Social constructionism</b></span> says that reality is created through <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>social interactions</u></span> &amp; <i>maintained</i> through <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>agreed-upon meanings</u></span>.

"<b>Normative influence</b> happens mostly between individuals that are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;similar&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;to&#x20;each&#x20;other"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<b>Normative influence</b> happens mostly between individuals that are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>similar</i> to each other</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This happens when we conform in order to be liked by others.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-a93f62d215cbbe40704ab522b031326217c8cb53.jpg""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Compliance</b></span> is the lowest form of conformity when we <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""maintain&#x20;our&#x20;own&#x20;&lt;u&gt;private&#x20;beliefs&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> but <i>publicly</i> conform to the group.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Compliance</b></span> is the lowest form of conformity when we <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">maintain our own <u>private beliefs</u></span> but <i>publicly</i> conform to the group.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""01-types-of-conformity-power-point-7-638.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Groupthink</b></span> is when groups reach <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>irrational conclusions</u></span> due to its members <i>failing</i> to think critically on the issue because they care more about <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""maintaining&#x20;group&#x20;harmony"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Groupthink</b></span> is when groups reach <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>irrational conclusions</u></span> due to its members <i>failing</i> to think critically on the issue because they care more about <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">maintaining group harmony</span>.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""business-meetings.jpg""></div>"

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Social mobility</b></span> refers to a person's ability to move <i>between</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>social statuses</u></span>.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Social mobility</b></span> refers to a person's ability to move <i>between</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>social statuses</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Ex: transitioning from middle-class to upper class.<div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-759dd03a6cd019197ecb48d3d2dba30642b53758.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"What's the difference between the actor-observer bias &amp; the fundamental attribution bias?<div><br></div><div><b>Actor-observer bias</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""failures&#x20;from&#x20;others&#x20;are&#x20;due&#x20;to&#x20;their&#x20;internal&#x20;characteristics&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&#x20;&lt;i&gt;our&#x20;own&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;failures&#x20;are&#x20;due&#x20;to&#x20;external&#x20;factors&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div><b>Fundamental attribution error</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""behaviors&#x20;from&#x20;others&#x20;are&#x20;due&#x20;to&#x20;their&#x20;internal&#x20;characteristics&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"What's the difference between the actor-observer bias &amp; the fundamental attribution bias?<div><br></div><div><b>Actor-observer bias</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">failures from others are due to their internal characteristics &amp; <i>our own</i> failures are due to external factors.</span></div><div><b>Fundamental attribution error</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">behaviors from others are due to their internal characteristics.</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Actor-observer bias</b> takes into account <u>our own</u> actions (unlike fundamental attribution error).</div><div><br></div><img src=""de19db609180f2ff76fc386290a9ee1c.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Self&#x2D;discrepancy&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span> says people compare their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>actual-selves</u></span> to their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ideal-selves</u></span> and experience <i>discomfort</i> when the two don't align."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Self-discrepancy theory</b></span> says people compare their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>actual-selves</u></span> to their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ideal-selves</u></span> and experience <i>discomfort</i> when the two don't align.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Actual-self</b>: who you truly&nbsp;<u>are</u>.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Ideal-self</b>: what you really&nbsp;<u>want</u>&nbsp;to be.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Ought-self</b>: version of yourself you feel you&nbsp;<u>should</u>&nbsp;be, often instilled by other people.</div><div>- Created by&nbsp;Edward Higgins.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-6dec0eeeaf401b607c38d9dbf9d3de861a0415a2.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Self-discrepancy theory</b></span> says people compare their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;actual&#x2D;selves&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;ideal&#x2D;selves&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> and experience <i>discomfort</i> when the two don't align."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Self-discrepancy theory</b></span> says people compare their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>actual-selves</u></span> to their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ideal-selves</u></span> and experience <i>discomfort</i> when the two don't align.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Actual-self</b>: who you truly&nbsp;<u>are</u>.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Ideal-self</b>: what you really&nbsp;<u>want</u>&nbsp;to be.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Ought-self</b>: version of yourself you feel you&nbsp;<u>should</u>&nbsp;be, often instilled by other people.</div><div>- Created by&nbsp;Edward Higgins.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-6dec0eeeaf401b607c38d9dbf9d3de861a0415a2.jpg""></div>"
"In <u>social-discrepancy theory</u>, your <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ideal&#x2D;self&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the version of yourself you <i>want</i> to be."	"In <u>social-discrepancy theory</u>, your <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ideal-self</b></span> is the version of yourself you <i>want</i> to be.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Actual-self</b>: who you truly <u>are</u>.</div><div>- <b>Ideal-self</b>: what you really <u>want</u> to be.</div><div>- <b>Ought-self</b>: version of yourself you feel you <u>should</u> be, often instilled by other people.</div><div>- Created by Edward Higgins.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-6dec0eeeaf401b607c38d9dbf9d3de861a0415a2.jpg""></div>"
"In <u>social-discrepancy theory</u>, your <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ought&#x2D;self&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the version of youself you feel you <i>should</i> be - instilled by <u>other people's opinions</u>."	"In <u>social-discrepancy theory</u>, your <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ought-self</b></span> is the version of youself you feel you <i>should</i> be - instilled by <u>other people's opinions</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Actual-self</b>: who you truly <u>are</u>.</div><div>- <b>Ideal-self</b>: what you really <u>want</u> to be.</div><div>- <b>Ought-self</b>: version of yourself you feel you <u>should</u> be, often instilled by other people.</div><div>- Created by Edward Higgins.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-6dec0eeeaf401b607c38d9dbf9d3de861a0415a2.jpg""></div>"

"In <u>social-discrepancy theory</u>, your <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>actual-self</b></span> is who you truly are.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Actual-self</b>: who you truly <u>are</u>.</div><div>- <b>Ideal-self</b>: what you really <u>want</u> to be.</div><div>- <b>Ought-self</b>: version of yourself you feel you <u>should</u> be, often instilled by other people.</div><div>- Created by Edward Higgins.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-6dec0eeeaf401b607c38d9dbf9d3de861a0415a2.jpg""></div>"
"<u>Learned helplessness</u> (believing you're destined for failure) arises from an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;external&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[internal/external]</span> <b>locus of control</b>."	"<u>Learned helplessness</u> (believing you're destined for failure) arises from an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>external</i></span> <b>locus of control</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Believe you're <u>destined for failure</u> due to things outside of your own control.</div><div><br></div><img src=""what-is-learned-helplessness-2795326_final-64957db26aeb4c9e8621058b8995e967.png""></div>"
"The <b>self-discrepancy theory</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;cannot&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[can/cannot]</span> be used to describe the formation of our <u>self-identity</u>."	"The <b>self-discrepancy theory</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>cannot</i></span> be used to describe the formation of our <u>self-identity</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Instead, this only describes the <u>discomfort</u> we feel when our ideal-self doesn't align with our real-self.</div><div><br></div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Actual-self</b>: who you truly&nbsp;<u>are</u>.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Ideal-self</b>: what you really&nbsp;<u>want</u>&nbsp;to be.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Ought-self</b>: version of yourself you feel you&nbsp;<u>should</u>&nbsp;be, often instilled by other people.</div><div>- Created by Edward Higgins.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-6dec0eeeaf401b607c38d9dbf9d3de861a0415a2.jpg""></div>"
"According to Mead, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;generalized&#x20;other&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is our understanding of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>society as a whole</u></span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-a1fc3e19e4f93f95fc9e51439743e9eec36a694f.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-398e4aa0d118a1368355fb09935dab9a486824a5.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>groupthink</u></span>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;illusion&#x20;of&#x20;morality&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when group members believe they are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">morally superior</span> to their competition - making them ignore the consequences of their <i>own</i> decisions." "In <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;groupthink&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[social phenomenon]</span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>illusion of morality</b></span> is when group members believe they are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">morally superior</span> to their competition - making them ignore the consequences of their <i>own</i> decisions." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-a1fc3e19e4f93f95fc9e51439743e9eec36a694f.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-398e4aa0d118a1368355fb09935dab9a486824a5.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>groupthink</u></span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>illusion of morality</b></span> is when group members believe they are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""morally&#x20;superior"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> to their competition - making them ignore the consequences of their <i>own</i> decisions."	"In <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>groupthink</u></span>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>illusion of morality</b></span> is when group members believe they are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">morally superior</span> to their competition - making them ignore the consequences of their <i>own</i> decisions.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-a1fc3e19e4f93f95fc9e51439743e9eec36a694f.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-398e4aa0d118a1368355fb09935dab9a486824a5.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;correspondent&#x20;inference&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span> explains our <i>tendency</i> to attribute the behavior of others as either <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>dispositional</u></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>situational</u></span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>correspondent inference theory</b></span> explains our <i>tendency</i> to attribute the behavior of others as either <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>dispositional</u></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>situational</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- We're more likely to use <b>dispositional attribution</b> (""it's his internal character"") to explain behaviors that either directly help or hurt us or when it's different from normal behavior in society.</div><div>- We're more likely to use <b>situational attribution</b> to explain behaviors we see as being accidental.</div><div><br></div><img src=""de19db609180f2ff76fc386290a9ee1c.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>correspondent inference theory</b></span> explains our <i>tendency</i> to attribute the behavior of others as either <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;dispositional&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;situational&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>correspondent inference theory</b></span> explains our <i>tendency</i> to attribute the behavior of others as either <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>dispositional</u></span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>situational</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- We're more likely to use <b>dispositional attribution</b> (""it's his internal character"") to explain behaviors that either directly help or hurt us or when it's different from normal behavior in society.</div><div>- We're more likely to use <b>situational attribution</b> to explain behaviors we see as being accidental.</div><div><br></div><img src=""de19db609180f2ff76fc386290a9ee1c.png""></div>"
"<div>According to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""correspondent&#x20;inference&#x20;theory"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span>, we're <i>more</i> likely to use <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>dispositional</b></span> <b>attribution</b> to explain the behaviors of others that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>either <i>directly</i> help or harm us</u></span>, or when it <i>differs</i> from typical behavior.</div>"	"<div>According to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">correspondent inference theory</span>, we're <i>more</i> likely to use <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>dispositional</b></span> <b>attribution</b> to explain the behaviors of others that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>either <i>directly</i> help or harm us</u></span>, or when it <i>differs</i> from typical behavior.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- We're more likely to use <b>dispositional attribution</b> (""it's just who he is"") to explain behaviors that either directly help or hurt us or when it's different from normal behavior in society.</div><div>- We're more likely to use <b>situational attribution</b> to explain behaviors we see as being accidental.</div><div><br></div><img src=""de19db609180f2ff76fc386290a9ee1c.png""></div>"
"<div>According to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">correspondent inference theory</span>, we're <i>more</i> likely to use <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;dispositional&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <b>attribution</b> to explain the behaviors of others that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>either <i>directly</i> help or harm us</u></span>, or when it <i>differs</i> from typical behavior.</div>"	"<div>According to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">correspondent inference theory</span>, we're <i>more</i> likely to use <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>dispositional</b></span> <b>attribution</b> to explain the behaviors of others that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>either <i>directly</i> help or harm us</u></span>, or when it <i>differs</i> from typical behavior.</div>

<div>- Correspondent inference theory explains our <u>tendency to attribute</u> the behaviors of other as either <b>dispositional</b> or <b>situational</b>.</div><div><br></div><div>- We're more likely to use <b>dispositional attribution</b> (""it's just who he is"") to explain behaviors that either directly help or hurt us or when it's different from normal behavior in society.</div><div>- We're more likely to use <b>situational attribution</b> to explain behaviors we see as being accidental.</div><div><br></div><img src=""de19db609180f2ff76fc386290a9ee1c.png""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Correspondent inference theory explains our <u>tendency to attribute</u> the behaviors of other as either <b>dispositional</b> or <b>situational</b>.</div><div><br></div><div>- We're more likely to use <b>dispositional attribution</b> (""it's just who he is"") to explain behaviors that either directly help or hurt us or when it's different from normal behavior in society.</div><div>- We're more likely to use <b>situational attribution</b> to explain behaviors we see as being accidental.</div><div><br></div><img src=""de19db609180f2ff76fc386290a9ee1c.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;defensive&#x20;attribution&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[theory]</span> says that we <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>blame the victims</u>&nbsp;of serious accidents</span> in order to make ourselves feel like <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">it could <i>never</i> happen to <i>us</i></span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>defensive attribution theory</b></span> says that we <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>blame the victims</u>&nbsp;of serious accidents</span> in order to make ourselves feel like <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">it could <i>never</i> happen to <i>us</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Draw distinctions between yourself and the victim in order to make yourself feel like it would never happen to you (because you do something differently).</div><div>- ""Well look at what she was wearing!"" → ""that would never happen to me because I would never wear that.""</div><div>- Does not usually happen when people are significantly different from us, because it'd be less of a ""personal threat"" against us.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""de19db609180f2ff76fc386290a9ee1c.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>defensive attribution theory</b></span> says that we <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;blame&#x20;the&#x20;victims&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;of&#x20;serious&#x20;accidents"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in order to make ourselves feel like <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""it&#x20;could&#x20;&lt;i&gt;never&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;happen&#x20;to&#x20;&lt;i&gt;us&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- This is a more mild form of PTSD.</div>- Spawns from a traumatic event.<div>- Difference between PTSD &amp; ASD: ASD can be diagnosed sooner than a month after occurrence of the event (unlike PTSD).<br><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-188d1797ffb65276aa9cca945913bf5e11ab2496.jpg""><br></div></div></div>"
"Individuals with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;personality&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[mental/personality]</span> <u>disorders</u> are usually <b>ego</b>-<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>syntonic</b></span> - they believe there's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>nothing</i> out of the ordinary</span> about their thoughts/behavior."	"Individuals with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>personality</u></span> <u>disorders</u> are usually <b>ego</b>-<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>syntonic</b></span> - they believe there's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>nothing</i> out of the ordinary</span> about their thoughts/behavior.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Ego-syntonic</b>: they lack the self-awareness to see how their behavior is abnormal - assume ""that's just normal behavior (personality disorders).<br></div><div>- <b>Ego-dystonic</b>: you're able to recognize that you feel out of the ordinary - not how you normally feel (mental disorders).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ef1f76ddb4f05a76fc4a1dbc8ebf12d9d5adc416.jpg""></div>"
"Individuals with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>personality</u></span> <u>disorders</u> are usually <b>ego</b>-<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;syntonic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> - they believe there's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;nothing&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;out&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;ordinary"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> about their thoughts/behavior."	"Individuals with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>personality</u></span> <u>disorders</u> are usually <b>ego</b>-<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>syntonic</b></span> - they believe there's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>nothing</i> out of the ordinary</span> about their thoughts/behavior.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Ego-syntonic</b>: they lack the self-awareness to see how their behavior is abnormal - assume ""that's just normal behavior (personality disorders).<br></div><div>- <b>Ego-dystonic</b>: you're able to recognize that you feel out of the ordinary - not how you normally feel (mental disorders).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ef1f76ddb4f05a76fc4a1dbc8ebf12d9d5adc416.jpg""></div>"
"Individuals with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;mental&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[mental/personality]</span> <u>disorders</u> are usually <b>ego</b>-<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>dystonic</b></span> - they're <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>able</i> to recognize</span> they feel different than usual."	"Individuals with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>mental</u></span> <u>disorders</u> are usually <b>ego</b>-<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>dystonic</b></span> - they're <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>able</i> to recognize</span> they feel different than usual.

<div>Salesman: ""since you two are both clearly health-conscious, you should be very interested in this probiotic.""</div><div><br></div><div><div>-&nbsp;Apart of the&nbsp;<u>dramaturgical approach</u>&nbsp;outlined by&nbsp;<b>Erving Goffman</b>.</div><div><br></div><div>Impression management tehcniques...</div><div>- Self-disclosure: describing to other people what makes you unique.</div><div>- Managing appearances: trying to fit in.</div><div>- Altercasting: projecting an identity onto someone else to get them to agree with you.</div><div>- Aligning actions: make excuses for your questionable&nbsp;behavior to make it seem more acceptable to others.</div><div>- Ingration: winning people over through flattery or conformity.</div><div><br></div><img src=""392db704f3a56cdddc47a8a14bed6a42.png""><br></div></div>"

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Absolute&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>poverty</b> is defined as the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>inability</i> to secure basic needs</u></span>&nbsp;- <i>irrespective</i> of comparisons made to others."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Absolute</b></span> <b>poverty</b> is defined as the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>inability</i> to secure basic needs</u></span>&nbsp;- <i>irrespective</i> of comparisons made to others.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Absolute poverty</b>: <u>does not</u> make comparisons to other households.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Relative poverty</b>: <u>does</u> make comparisons to other households.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-ce000fced027e1e8cdfc492aab36b65e7df7ce2d.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Absolute</b></span> <b>poverty</b> is defined as the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;inability&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;to&#x20;secure&#x20;basic&#x20;needs&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>&nbsp;- <i>irrespective</i> of comparisons made to others."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Absolute</b></span> <b>poverty</b> is defined as the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>inability</i> to secure basic needs</u></span>&nbsp;- <i>irrespective</i> of comparisons made to others.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Absolute poverty</b>: <u>does not</u> make comparisons to other households.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Relative poverty</b>: <u>does</u> make comparisons to other households.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-ce000fced027e1e8cdfc492aab36b65e7df7ce2d.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Relative&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>poverty</b> is defined as being <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>below</i> 50% of the median income</span> in a country."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Relative</b></span> <b>poverty</b> is defined as being <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>below</i> 50% of the median income</span> in a country.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Absolute poverty</b>: <u>does not</u> make comparisons to other households.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Relative poverty</b>: <u>does</u> make comparisons to other households.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-ce000fced027e1e8cdfc492aab36b65e7df7ce2d.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Relative</b></span> <b>poverty</b> is defined as being <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;below&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;50&#x25;&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;median&#x20;income"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in a country."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Relative</b></span> <b>poverty</b> is defined as being <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>below</i> 50% of the median income</span> in a country.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Absolute poverty</b>: <u>does not</u> make comparisons to other households.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Relative poverty</b>: <u>does</u> make comparisons to other households.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-ce000fced027e1e8cdfc492aab36b65e7df7ce2d.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"What's the <i>difference</i> between how <u>absolute poverty</u> &amp; <u>relative poverty</u> are determined?<div><br></div><div><b>Absolute poverty</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;does&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;make&#x20;comparisons&#x20;between&#x20;households&#x20;&#x28;only&#x20;looks&#x20;at&#x20;the&#x20;ability&#x20;to&#x20;secure&#x20;basic&#x20;needs&#x29;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div><b>Relative poverty</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;makes&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;&lt;u&gt;comparisons&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;between&#x20;different&#x20;households&#x20;&#x28;50&#x25;&#x20;of&#x20;median&#x20;income&#x29;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"What's the <i>difference</i> between how <u>absolute poverty</u> &amp; <u>relative poverty</u> are determined?<div><br></div><div><b>Absolute poverty</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>does not</i></u> make comparisons between households (only looks at the ability to secure basic needs).</span></div><div><b>Relative poverty</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>makes</i> <u>comparisons</u> between different households (50% of median income).</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Absolute poverty</b>: <u>does not</u> make comparisons to other households.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Relative poverty</b>: <u>does</u> make comparisons to other households.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-ce000fced027e1e8cdfc492aab36b65e7df7ce2d.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"A <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;caste&#x20;system&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is a type of societal structuring where individuals are <i>ascribed</i> <u>hierarchical categories</u> and are <i>limited</i> in their social mobility."	"A <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>caste system</b></span> is a type of societal structuring where individuals are <i>ascribed</i> <u>hierarchical categories</u> and are <i>limited</i> in their social mobility.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-5216247f52468865a24c4adc75bf09a1a0e30c28.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-d3101b2a61a7c81291553eec3cbc95f456bcc80f.jpg""></div>"
"<b>Borderline</b> individuals <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;do&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[do/do not]</span> experience <u>depression</u>."	"<b>Borderline</b> individuals <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>do not</i></span> experience <u>depression</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Even though they experience thoughts of self-harm or suicide, it's due more to impulsive/reckless behavior as opposed to being caused by depression.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">Cluster B</b><br></div><div>- Primarily involves extreme emotional reactivity and impulsive behavior...&nbsp;<u>not</u>&nbsp;depression.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Screen Shot 2019-04-10 at 9.51.19 PM.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Liviasoprano.jpg""></div></div>"
"<u>Fear of abandonment</u> is common in individuals with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;borderline&#x20;personality&#x20;disorder&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[personality disorder]</span>."	"<u>Fear of abandonment</u> is common in individuals with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>borderline personality disorder</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b style=""text-decoration-line: underline;"">Cluster B</b><br></div><div>- Primarily involves extreme emotional reactivity and impulsive behavior...&nbsp;<u>not</u>&nbsp;depression.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Screen Shot 2019-04-10 at 9.51.19 PM.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Liviasoprano.jpg""></div></div>"
"According to the <b>world-systems theory</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;periphery&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;semi&#x2D;periphery&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;countries <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">export <i>resources</i></span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;core&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> countries."	"According to the <b>world-systems theory</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>periphery</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>semi-periphery</u></span>&nbsp;countries <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">export <i>resources</i></span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>core</u></span> countries.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Core</b>&nbsp;countries → export&nbsp;<u>goods</u>&nbsp;to periphery &amp; semi-periphery.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Periphery/semi-periphery</b>&nbsp;countries → export&nbsp;<u>resources</u>&nbsp;to core countries.</div></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-617d4572b43baf942748d4d9a6f8013c903bee97.jpg""></div>"
"According to the <b>world-systems theory</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>periphery</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>semi-periphery</u></span>&nbsp;countries <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""export&#x20;&lt;i&gt;resources&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>core</u></span> countries."	"According to the <b>world-systems theory</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>periphery</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>semi-periphery</u></span>&nbsp;countries <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">export <i>resources</i></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>core</u></span> countries.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Core</b>&nbsp;countries → export&nbsp;<u>goods</u>&nbsp;to periphery &amp; semi-periphery.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Periphery/semi-periphery</b>&nbsp;countries → export&nbsp;<u>resources</u>&nbsp;to core countries.</div></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-617d4572b43baf942748d4d9a6f8013c903bee97.jpg""></div>"
"According to the <b>world-systems theory</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;core&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> countries <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">export <i>goods</i></span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;periphery&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;semi&#x2D;periphery&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> countries."	"According to the <b>world-systems theory</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>core</u></span> countries <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">export <i>goods</i></span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>periphery</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>semi-periphery</u></span> countries.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Core</b> countries → export <u>goods</u> to periphery &amp; semi-periphery.</div><div>- <b>Periphery/semi-periphery</b> countries → export <u>resources</u> to core countries.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-617d4572b43baf942748d4d9a6f8013c903bee97.jpg""></div>"
"According to the <b>world-systems theory</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>core</u></span> countries <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""export&#x20;&lt;i&gt;goods&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>periphery</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>semi-periphery</u></span> countries."	"According to the <b>world-systems theory</b>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>core</u></span> countries <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">export <i>goods</i></span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>periphery</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>semi-periphery</u></span> countries.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Core</b> countries → export <u>goods</u> to periphery &amp; semi-periphery.</div><div>- <b>Periphery/semi-periphery</b> countries → export <u>resources</u> to core countries.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-617d4572b43baf942748d4d9a6f8013c903bee97.jpg""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;teacher&#x20;expectancy&#x20;effect&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> describes how the expectations set by a <u>teacher</u> on a <u>student</u> tends to result in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">the <i>expected</i> behavior</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>teacher expectancy effect</b></span> describes how the expectations set by a <u>teacher</u> on a <u>student</u> tends to result in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">the <i>expected</i> behavior</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Teachers have different expectations of students results in them unconsciously treating students differently.</div><div><br></div><div>- ""He's a good student!"" → student performs better.</div><div>- ""He's a bad student!"" → student performs worse.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-15f5f400308638792ea507adfba2d018c74e53d3.jpg""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>teacher expectancy effect</b></span> describes how the expectations set by a <u>teacher</u> on a <u>student</u> tends to result in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""the&#x20;&lt;i&gt;expected&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;behavior"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>teacher expectancy effect</b></span> describes how the expectations set by a <u>teacher</u> on a <u>student</u> tends to result in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">the <i>expected</i> behavior</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Teachers have different expectations of students results in them unconsciously treating students differently.</div><div><br></div><div>- ""He's a good student!"" → student performs better.</div><div>- ""He's a bad student!"" → student performs worse.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-15f5f400308638792ea507adfba2d018c74e53d3.jpg""></div>"
"<b>Exchange theory</b> is <i>also</i> called <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;social&#x20;exchange&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<b>Exchange theory</b> is <i>also</i> called <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>social exchange theory</b></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This says that we act to maximize personal gain &amp; minimize loss in social settings (similar to <b>rational-choice theory</b>).</div><div><br></div><img src=""4yDQBLS.png""><div></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;life&#x20;course&#x20;theory&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> views the process of aging <i>holistically</i> - looks at the effects of aging on <u>society</u>, <u>culture</u>, <u>biology</u>, <u>psychology</u>, etc."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>life course theory</b></span> views the process of aging <i>holistically</i> - looks at the effects of aging on <u>society</u>, <u>culture</u>, <u>biology</u>, <u>psychology</u>, etc.

"In the <u>Malthusian theory</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Malthusian catastrophes</b></span> are large-scale <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>positive</i></span> checks, like disease or war.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Theory that says our population will eventually reach a point where society is unable to feed everyone - resulting in a catastrophe (crisis).</div><div>- <u>Positive</u> checks: Increase the death rate (disease, war).</div><div>- <u>Preventative</u> checks: Decrease the birth rate.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-8f89c615d8171103257116e317352229efa9f4ad.jpg""></div>"
"In the <u>Malthusian theory</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Malthusian catastrophes</b></span> are large-scale <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;positive&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> checks, like disease or war."	"In the <u>Malthusian theory</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Malthusian catastrophes</b></span> are large-scale <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>positive</i></span> checks, like disease or war.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Theory that says our population will eventually reach a point where society is unable to feed everyone - resulting in a catastrophe (crisis).</div><div>- <u>Positive</u> checks: Increase the death rate (disease, war).</div><div>- <u>Preventative</u> checks: Decrease the birth rate.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-8f89c615d8171103257116e317352229efa9f4ad.jpg""></div>"
"In terms of <u>population fertility</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;crude&#x20;birth&#x20;rate&#x20;&#x28;CBR&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> refers to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>number</i> of live births per 1,000 women</span> per year."	"In terms of <u>population fertility</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>crude birth rate (CBR)</b></span> refers to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>number</i> of live births per 1,000 women</span> per year.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Crude birth rate (CBR)</b>: number of live births per year per 1,000 individuals.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Total fertility rate (TFR)</b>: average number of children born to a women in her lifetime.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>General fertility rate (GFR)</b>: total number of live births per year for 1,000 child-bearing aged women.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Age-specific fertility rate (ASFR)</b>: number of births per 1,000 women of a specific age-group.</div><b><div><b><br></b></div>Fertility</b>: ability for humans to actually give birth (per 1,000 individuals).<div><b>Fecundity</b>: reproductive&nbsp;<u>potential</u>&nbsp;to have offspring of one single female.</div><div><br></div><div>- This&nbsp;<i><u>is</u></i>&nbsp;factored into population dynamics (fecundity is not).</div></div>"
"In terms of <u>population fertility</u>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>crude birth rate (CBR)</b></span> refers to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;number&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;of&#x20;live&#x20;births&#x20;per&#x20;1&#x2C;000&#x20;women"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> per year."	"In terms of <u>population fertility</u>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>crude birth rate (CBR)</b></span> refers to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>number</i> of live births per 1,000 women</span> per year.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-a310319f61c2db6a0dd37eff9a5d9bf43276d934.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""5f7c22748ed67ff0a6e2dfffeece97cc.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Self</b></span> <b>stigma </b>is when an individual <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;internalizes&lt;i&gt;&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;the&#x20;stigma&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <i>attributed</i> to them by <i>other</i> people - leading to avoidance, depression, isolation.<br>"	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Self</b></span> <b>stigma </b>is when an individual <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>internalizes<i> </i>the stigma</u></span> <i>attributed</i> to them by <i>other</i> people - leading to avoidance, depression, isolation.<br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Other people are the ones that attribute the stigma to you - you just happen to internalize it.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""bdc88c6e150d14664d97e888ecf90435.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"In Merton's strain theory, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ritualism&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when people <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>reject</i> conventional goals</u> (like getting rich)</span>&nbsp;but <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>still</i>&nbsp;follow the conventional means</u></span> to getting there (working)."	"In Merton's strain theory, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ritualism</b></span> is when people <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>reject</i> conventional goals</u> (like getting rich)</span>&nbsp;but <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>still</i>&nbsp;follow the conventional means</u></span> to getting there (working).

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<hr>
